## PAGE 01144: (2026x) Resource Interface

- page_id: `254439214`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554850866

554850868

554850867

##### Description

UPDM: ResourceInterface is a contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source resource to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is consumed by the other (DoDAF::Interface).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Association

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

## PAGE 01145: (2026x) Resource Message

- page_id: `254439290`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439290/2026x+Resource+Message
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:54.915+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554913424

554913426

554913425

##### Description

UPDM: Message for use in a Resource Event-Trace, implements a ResourceInteraction.

MODAF/NAF: A specification of the interactions between aspects of a Resources architecture (MODAF::ResourceInteractionSpecification, NAF::ResourceInteractionSpecification).

DoDAF: An overlap of an Activity with a Resource, in particular a consuming or producing Activity that expresses an input, output, consumption, or production Activity of the Resource (DoDAF::ActivityResourceOverlap).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Message

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-10c+Resource+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description)

## PAGE 01148: (2026x) Resource Port

- page_id: `254439260`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

555062006

555062008

555062007

##### Description

UPDM: Port is an interaction point for a resource through which it can interact with the outside environment.

MODAF/NAF: An interface (logical or physical) provided by a System. A SystemPort may implement a PortType though there is no requirement for SystemPorts to be typed (MODAF::SystemPort, NAF::SystemPort).

DoDAF: An interface (logical or physical) provided by a System (DoDAF::Port).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

[SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-2+Systems+Internal+Resource+Flow+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

[MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-1 System Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSV-1+System+Interface+Description)

[NSV-2 System Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSV-2+System+Communications+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439052 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01153: (2026x) Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Objects

- page_id: `254439052`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439052/2026x+Reusing+Measurement+Sets+for+Actual+Objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:33.053+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['reuse-measurement-sets-for-actual-objects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a Node Role or Resource Role, you can quickly reuse the Measurement Sets of their type (Node or System Resource). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Measurement Sets is displayed in the Specification window of an element for which you are creating the Actual Measurement Set.

You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurement Sets when creating the following actual objects:

- Node Role
- Resource Role
- Operational Activity Action
- Function Action
- Instances

When creating instances, the Actual Measurement Sets belonging to the classifier are copied automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The type and behavior measurements in the Actual Object Measurements compartment are displayed *Italic*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Measurement Sets is displayed in the Specification window of an element for which you are creating the Actual Measurement Set.</p><p>You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurement Sets when creating the following actual objects:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Node Role</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Resource Role</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Operational Activity Action</li><li>Function Action</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Instances</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When creating instances, the Actual Measurement Sets belonging to the classifier are copied automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type and behavior measurements in the Actual Object Measurements compartment are displayed <em>Italic</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01155: (2026x) Rule

- page_id: `254439584`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439584/2026x+Rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:24.207+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1101034189

1101034191

1101034190

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An abstract Class that is extended by OperationalConstraint (A rule governing an operational behaviour or property.) and ResourceConstraint (A rule governing the structural or functional aspects of an implementation - this may also include constraints on OrganisationalResources that are part of an implementation).

DoDAF: Rule: A principle or condition that governs behavior; a prescribed guide for conduct or action. Subtype: Constraint: The range of permissible states for an object.

Rule is abstract.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Constraint

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-6a Operational Rules Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439232 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01156: (2026x) S1 Service Taxonomy

- page_id: `254439232`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439232/2026x+S1+Service+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:49.026+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s1-service-taxonomy', 's1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.

A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.

The S1 view may be used to:

- SOA governance.
- Identification of services.
- Service planning.
- Service audit.
- Service gap analysis.
- Providing reference services for architectures.
- Tailoring generic services for specific applications.

**Implementation**

The S1 view can be represented using:

- A S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1.png']
- A C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1_matrix.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating S1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating C1-S1 matrix' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Capability
- Expose

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.</p><p><span>A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.</span></p><p>The S1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>SOA governance.</li><li>Identification of services.</li><li>Service planning.</li><li>Service audit.</li><li>Service gap analysis.</li><li>Providing reference services for architectures.</li><li>Tailoring generic services for specific applications.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S1 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt=" S1 Service Taxonomy diagram" ac:title=" S1 Service Taxonomy diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S1 Service Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>A C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix" ac:title="S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S1_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S1 Service Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating S1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating C1-S1 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interface</li><li>Capability</li><li>Expose</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439536 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01157: (2026x) S3 Service Interfaces

- page_id: `254439536`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439536/2026x+S3+Service+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:16.637+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s3-service-interfaces', 's3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S3 Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.

**Implementation**

The S3 view can be represented using:

- A S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S3.png']
- A S3 MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Service Operation
- Service Attribute
- Service Parameter
- Service
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.</p><p>Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S3 Service Interfaces diagram" ac:title="S3 Service Interfaces diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S3 Service Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="114de996-cdfe-4074-886b-5de4d3ff8db8">A S3 MS Word document report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service Operation</li><li>Service Attribute</li><li>Service Parameter</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439400 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01158: (2026x) S4 Service Functions

- page_id: `254439400`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439400/2026x+S4+Service+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:03.122+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s4-service-functions', 's4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S4 Service Functions view defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. S4 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L4 Logical Activities' space=''] and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

**Implementation**

The S4 view can be represented using:

- A S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4.png']

- A S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4_service_functions.png']
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating S4 Service Functions diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Function
- Service Operation Action
- Service Function Action
- Function Edge
- Service Operation
- Service Parameter
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Equivalent in nature to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4 - Logical Activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S4 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram" ac:title="S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S4 Service Functions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>A S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. ## PAGE 01159: (2026x) S5 Service States

- page_id: `254439550`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439550/2026x+S5+Service+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:18.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s5-service-states', 's5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The S5 view can be represented using a S5 Service States diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service State Machine
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S5 view can be represented using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee4b8256-c894-4fc6-aa98-222af27fda31">S5 Service States diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S5 Service States diagram" ac:title="S5 Service States diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S5 Service States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service State Machine</li><li>Service Interface</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439543 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01160: (2026x) S6 Service Interactions

- page_id: `254439543`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439543/2026x+S6+Service+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:17.476+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s6-service-interactions', 's6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) L6 Logical Sequence' space='']). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interaction
- Service Message
- Service Interface
- Service Attribute

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L6 Logical Sequence" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L6 - Logical Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="S6 Service Interactions diagram" ac:title="S6 Service Interactions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) S6 Service Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interaction</li><li>Service Message</li><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service Attribute</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S7 Service I/F Parameters view identifies all the parameters used in Service Interfaces (see also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) S3 Service Interfaces' space='']).

**Implementation**

The S7 view can be represented using S7 Service I/F Parameters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Service Operation
- Service Attribute
- Service Parameter
- Service
- Request

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439420 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01162: (2026x) S8 Service Policy

- page_id: `254439420`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439420/2026x+S8+Service+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:04.043+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s8-service-policy', 's8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S8 Service Policy view is to specify constraints that apply to implementations of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.

**Implementation**

The S8 view can be represented using:

- A S8 Service Policy table. [ATTACHMENT filename='S8.png']
- A S8 spreadsheet report .

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating S8 table' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Policy
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439925 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01166: (2026x) Select Symbol dialog

- page_id: `254439925`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439925/2026x+Select+Symbol+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:44.976+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Dialogs
- labels: ['select-symbol-dialog', 'affiliation', 'frame']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use **Select Symbol** dialog to apply military symbols for resources.

To open the **Select Symbol** dialog, do either

- Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
- Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
- Open the Specification window of any UPDM element. Select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] . The **Select Symbol** dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Select Symbol** dialog consists of these four components:

##### Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree

Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.

##### Quick filter

You can search for symbols in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs by using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy. For example, type "1.X.3.2.2.1.3".
- Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols. For example, type "*Frying" if you want to find "Fixed Wing" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Affiliation area

Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.

##### Frame area

Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.

##### Preview area

You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying military symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:alt="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology" ac:title="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />The <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog</span> opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog" ac:title="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Select Symbol dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog consists of these four components:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="6f3a7da5-9780-4043-9b1d-1479391dbc99" ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" /></p><h3>Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree</h3><p>Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.</p><h3>Quick filter</h3><p>You can search for symbols in the <strong>Whole hierarchy</strong> or <strong>Recently used</strong> tabs by using one of the following way:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy. </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="35bee693-e8a4-4b4e-8c4f-2bcc8ed0b2c3" ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;1.X.3.2.2.1.3&quot;.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="660312ee-045d-4875-a612-1aa2773946ac" ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;*Frying&quot; if you want to find &quot;Fixed Wing&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Affiliation area</span><br /></span></h3><p>Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.</p><h3>Frame area</h3><p>Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.</p><h3>Preview area</h3><p>You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1b223146-dd7b-4b24-b2a5-1bd0d50f609e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Applying military symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying military symbols ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

314459507

314459509

314459508

##### Description

A ServicePoint is the offer of a service by one participant to others using well defined terms, conditions and interfaces. A ServicePoint defines the connection point through which a Participant offers its capabilities and provides a service to clients.

##### Origin

SoaML

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

MODAF/NAF: A property of Service.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439185 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01171: (2026x) Service Channels Summary Matrix

- page_id: `254439185`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439185/2026x+Service+Channels+Summary+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:44.237+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['service-channels-summary-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Service Channel Summary matrix enables a quick overview of all the service interactions specified in one or more OV and SV models. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services.

The Service Channel Summary Matrix can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the Service Channel Summary Matrix includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

Service Channel Summary Matrix can be represented using a Service Channel Summary Matrix diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Channel
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request
- System
- Materiel
- Post

The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services.</p><p>The Service Channel Summary Matrix can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439201 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01172: (2026x) Service Channels Summary Table

- page_id: `254439201`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439201/2026x+Service+Channels+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:45.186+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['service-channels-summary-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Service Channel Summary Table specifies the characteristics of service resource flows between Services. The Service Channel Summary Table is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix and provides detailed information on the service connections which implement the Resource Flow exchanges specified in OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. According to the Net-Centric Data Strategy, a net-centric implementation needs to focus in on the data in the Service Resource Flow, as well as the services that produce or consume the data of the Service Resource Flow. In a net-centric implementation, not all the consumers are known and this model emphasizes the focus on the producer and Service Resource Flow.

**Implementation**

Service Channel Summary Table can be represented using:

- A Service Channel Summary Table diagram.
- A Service Channel Summary Table spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Channel
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request
- Expose
- Service Operation
- Service Parameter
- Performer
- System
- Materiel
- Capability Configuration
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01173: (2026x) Service Description

- page_id: `254439639`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439639/2026x+Service+Description

- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

244656554

244656557

244656555

##### Description

UPDM: Package containing the elements that describe a service, from DoDAF 2.

DoDAF: Information necessary to interact with the service in such terms as the service inputs, outputs, and associated semantics. The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Package</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439680 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 01174: (2026x) Service Function

- page_id: `254439680`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439680/2026x+Service+Function
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:43.460+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

232471216

232471219

232471218

##### Description

UPDM: A ServiceFunction describes the abstract behavior of ServiceOperations, regardless of the actual implementation.

MODAF/NAF: A type of activity describing the functionality of a service.

DoDAF: Information necessary to interact with the service in such terms as the service inputs, outputs, and associated semantics. The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Activity</span></p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description">SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description</a></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow">MODAF. ## PAGE 01175: (2026x) Service Function Action

- page_id: `254439646`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439646/2026x+Service+Function+Action
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:44.025+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

244523906

244523910

244523909

##### Description

UPDM: A call behavior action that invokes the ServiceFunction that needs to be preformed. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF or MODAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Behavior Action

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

245535463

245535465

245535464

##### Description

UPDM: Interaction for a service interface.

MODAF/NAF: A model representing how a set of Service classes interacts with one another (MODAF::ServiceInteractionSpecification, NAF::ServiceInteractionSpecification).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Interaction

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438650 space=UPDM2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 01177: (2026x) Service Interface

- page_id: `254438650`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438650/2026x+Service+Interface
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:45.189+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

246531099

246531102

246531101

##### Description

UPDM: A contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source service interacts to the destination resource. A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: The mechanism by which a Service communicates.

DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is consumed by the other (DoDAF::Interface).

SOAML: Defines the interface to a Service Point or Request Point and is the type of a role in a service contract.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| serviceInteraction : ServiceInteraction[0..1] | A Service interaction. |
| serviceStateMachine : ServiceStateMachine[0..1] | A Service state machine. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Description

UPDM: Message for use in a Service Interaction Specification, implements a resourceInteraction or any of the subtypes.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| carries : ResourceInteraction[*] | A Carried ResourceInteraction. |

##### Extensions

UML Message

## PAGE 01179: (2026x) Service Operation

- page_id: `254438683`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438683/2026x+Service+Operation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:46.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

249474688

249474690

249474689

##### Description

UPDM: A ServiceOperation provides the access point for invoking the behavior of a provided service. The ServiceOperations are defined on ServiceInterfaces and mirrored on the providing Resource to handle calls forwarded on by the interface.

MODAF/NAF: A function or procedure which enables programmatic communication with a Service via a ServiceInterface (MODAF::ServiceInterfaceOperation).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| abstractBehavior : ServiceFunction[0..1] | Links a ServiceOperation to the abstract description of its behavior, as provided by a ServiceFunction. |
| concreteBehavior : Function[0..1] | Links a ServiceOperation to the concrete description of its behavior, as provided by a Function. |

##### Extensions

UML Operation

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description)

[SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix)

[SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-2 Service Interface Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SOV-2+Service+Interface+Specification)

- page_id: `254438698`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

249789275

249789278

249789277

##### Description

UPDM Artifact: A call action that represents a Resource or Service Function invoking a ServiceOperation. This is used by a consuming Resource to model the call into the service. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF or MODAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Operation Action

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

MODAF/NAF: A constant or variable passed into or out of a ServiceInterface as part of the execution of a ServiceInterfaceOperation (MODAF::ServiceInterfaceParameter).

DoDAF: NA

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Description

UPDM: A constraint governing the consumers and providers of services. MODAF/NAF: A constraint governing one or more services.

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Constraint

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439230 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01184: (2026x) Service viewpoint

- page_id: `254439230`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439230/2026x+Service+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:48.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['service-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Service viewpoint is based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. It is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logical viewpoint' space='']. A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438601 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01185: (2026x) Services viewpoint

- page_id: `254438601`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438601/2026x+Services+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:55.270+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['services-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984167070

984167073

984167072

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

984167068

**Related Pages:**

These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c8ec0ed-c3d8-4b2f-a1f8-06546c9fdd3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984167068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="271fc43a-055c-4abe-84bd-35e55909a854" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439038 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01186: (2026x) Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets

- page_id: `254439038`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439038/2026x+Setting+up+Automatic+Population+of+Measurement+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:32.019+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['set-up-automatic-population-of-measurement-sets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time creating the Instance of the associated type. 
 
 To set up the automatic Measurement Set population

1. Create a Measurement Set.
2. Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.
3. Apply « DoDAF Properties» stereotype to the created Measurement Set.
4. Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the Expert properties mode is selected.
5. In the Tags property group, select the appliesFor and click the Create Value button.
6. Select the Exchange Element stereotype as the appliesFor tag value. [ATTACHMENT filename='exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png']
7. Save and reload the project.

You can see and edit your custom measurements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to see your measurements in a separate group within Exchange Element specification, add group name to the Measurement Set documentation field.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01190: (2026x) Standard

- page_id: `254438877`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438877/2026x+Standard
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:57.779+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Standards Viewpoint and Technical Standards Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

555942772

555942774

555942773

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture via the [constrainedItem] property of UML::Constraint.

DoDAF: A formal agreement documenting generally accepted specifications or criteria for products, processes, procedures, policies, systems, and/or personnel.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| InformationTechnologyStandardCategory: String[*] | The information technology standard category which the «Standard» belongs to. |
| currentStatus : String[1] | The current status of the Standard. |
| mandatedDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The date the version of the standard was published. |
| ratifiedBy : ActualOrganization[1] | The organization that ratified this Standard. |
| retiredDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The date the version of the standard was retired. |
| shortName : String[1] | The short name of the standard. |
| version : String[1] | Represents the revision number of the standard, e.g., “1.2.1”, “v2”, “:2004”, etc. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[StdV-1 Standards Profile](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) StdV-1+Standards+Profile)

[StdV-2 Standards Forecast](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) StdV-2+Standards+Forecast)

##### Related NAF views

555985777

555985779

555985778

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A UML::Comment that when attached to a CapabilityConfiguration indicates that it is a standard pattern for re-use in the architecture.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF/NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

## PAGE 01192: (2026x) Standard Operational Activity

- page_id: `254438902`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438902/2026x+Standard+Operational+Activity
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:16:26.625+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

916761723

916761725

916761724

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalActivity that is a standard procedure that is doctrinal.

This is equivalent to what some defense organizations call JETLs.

DoDAF: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual, that transforms inputs into outputs or changes their state (DoDAF::Activity).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) CV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438926 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01193: (2026x) Standards viewpoint

- page_id: `254438926`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438926/2026x+Standards+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:23.604+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['standards-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

#### PANEL: Views

Views

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="22683bb8-3816-47b8-8aa1-5062e78d5485" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="0036a364-4427-42db-8115-010b062161c8" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

##### Description

UPDM: Specifies a status for a ProjectTheme (such as training status).

MODAF/NAF: An enumeration of the possible statuses (MODAF::StatusIndicator, NAF::StatusIndicator) for one of more ProjectThemes.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Enumeration

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438937 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01195: (2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `254438937`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438937/2026x+StdV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:24.297+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-1-standards-profile', 'stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StdV-1 defines the technical, operational, and business standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture being described. As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.

In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. As with other models, each profile is assigned a specific timescale (e.g., “As-Is”, “To-Be”, or transitional). Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a StdV-2 Standards Forecast should be completed as well as a StdV-1.

The intended usage of the StdV-1 includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

StdV-1 can be represented by a StdV-1 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Standards cited are referenced as relationships to the systems, services, system functions, service functions, system data, service data, hardware/software items or communication protocols, where applicable, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Protocol

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438928 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01196: (2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `254438928`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438928/2026x+StdV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:23.701+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-2-standards-forecast', 'stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''] models.

A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01197: (2026x) Structural Part

- page_id: `254438960`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438960/2026x+Structural+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:25.674+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917035894

917035897

917035896

##### Description

UPDM: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. StructuralPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that describe the structure.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a spatial part of another, (MODAF::EnterpriseStructure).

This is a topological structuring relationship, hence the EnterprisePhase may be physically disjoint.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF Views

[MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision)

##### Related NAF Views

[NCV-1 Capability Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-1+Capability+Vision)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438711 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01199: (2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description

- page_id: `254438711`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438711/2026x+SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:07.669+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-description', 'sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of Systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of Performers - Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how Resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

A System Resource Flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all System Resource Flows between Systems that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between Systems may be further specified in detail in SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description and SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix.

Sub-System assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which Resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those Resources. In many cases, an operational activity and locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:

- Definition of System concepts.
- Definition of System options.
- System Resource Flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']).

The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SV-1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying military symbols' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Port
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Capability
- Capability Of Performer
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type
- Performer
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of Performers - Organizations and Personnel Types.</p><p>The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how Resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of Systems.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']).

The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438917 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01201: (2026x) SV-10a Systems Rules Model

- page_id: `254438917`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438917/2026x+SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:23.086+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10a-systems-rules-model', 'sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10a specifies functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e., the structural and behavioral elements of the Systems Viewpoint).

The SV-10a DoDAF-described Model describes constraints on the resources, functions, data, and ports that make up the SV. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).

The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

Implementation

SV-10a can be represented using:

- A SV-10a table.
- A SV-10a spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''], SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
