<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439608 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01202: (2026x) SV-10b Systems State Transition Description

- page_id: `254439608`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439608/2026x+SV-10b+Systems+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:25.669+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10b-systems-state-transition-description', 'sv-10b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or system function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ac:title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10b_sys_state_transit_desc.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="4faf469e-7ba5-4491-8de0-658c8bea2b51" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resources State Machine</li><li>Resource State</li><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438908 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01203: (2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `254438908`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438908/2026x+SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:22.586+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10c-systems-event-trace-description', 'sv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-10b Systems State Transition Description' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SV-10c may be related with Resource Flows (the interactions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='']), Resource Flows (the data in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='']) and entities (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='']) modeled in other models.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Interaction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ac:title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10c_sys_event_trace_desc.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SV-10c may be related with Resource Flows (the interactions in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3 Systems- Systems Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), Resource Flows (the data in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>) and entities (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Event Trace</li><li>Resource Message</li><li>Resource Interaction</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 01204: (2026x) SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `254439588`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439588/2026x+SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description+BPD

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

## PAGE 01205: (2026x) SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `254438725`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438725/2026x+SV-2+Systems+Internal+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:08.750+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-internal-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the internal System Resource Flows between Systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UM L Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''].

- Resource Interface

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438732 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01206: (2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `254438732`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438732/2026x+SV-2+Systems+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:09.422+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the System Resource Flows between Systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Systems. This may be an existing connection, or a specification for a connection that is to be made.

The intended usage of the SV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UM L Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying military symbols' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Protocol
- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Service
- Request
- Energy
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Geo Political Extent Type

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438763 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01207: (2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix

- page_id: `254438763`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438763/2026x+SV-3+Systems-Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:11.875+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-3-systems-systems-matrix', 'sv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-3 enables a quick overview of all the system resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] models. The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:

- Summarizing system resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The SV-3 matrix consists of as much rows and columns as there are Systems in the matrix data source.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] concentrates on System resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).</p><p>The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing system resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The SV-3 matrix consists of as much rows and columns as there are Systems in the matrix data source.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" ac:title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-3_sys_sys_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> concentrates on System resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="523c1e18-c73c-4063-9d04-dbebaca9cb45" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="53c68759-8d6c-4769-9627-66e017a7b6ea" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cdc42bb8-97bf-476b-8793-0968176d2e58" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Geo Political Extent Type</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01208: (2026x) SV-4 Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `254439509`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439509/2026x+SV-4+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:13.478+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438776 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01209: (2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description

- page_id: `254438776`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438776/2026x+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:12.670+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'sv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-4 addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of SV-4 are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Systems Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between functions.

The SV-4 is the Systems Viewpoint model counterpart to the OV-5b Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- A SV-4 diagram for System Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-4_hierarchy.png']
- An SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-4_flows.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']OV-5b Operational Activity Model is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Applying military symbols' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Activity Performed By Performer
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Resource Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-4 addresses human and system functionality.</p><p>The primary purposes of SV-4 are to:</p><ul><li>Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.</li><li>Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).</li><li>Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.</li></ul><p>The Systems Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:</p><ul><li>Allocation of functions to resources.</li><li>Flow of resources between functions.</li></ul><p>The SV-4 is the Systems Viewpoint model counterpart to the OV-5b Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Description of task workflow.</li><li>Identification of functional system requirements.</li><li>Functional decomposition of systems.</li><li>Relate human and system functions.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-4 diagram for System Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ac:title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-4_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description" ac:title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-4_flows.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Activity diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block diagram.</li><li>A SysML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p>The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> (in the same way that <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-5b Operational Activity Model</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> is the behavioral counterpart to <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="fda51c11-647d-4f9a-9a25-ef38952d67bd" ac:name="panel" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438818 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01210: (2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `254438818`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438818/2026x+SV-5a+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Function+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:16.660+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5a', 'sv-5a-operational-activity-to-systems-function-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-5a addresses the linkage between System Functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']. The SV-5a depicts the mapping of system functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. System Functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term System Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.

The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A System Function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='']. System Functions can be carried out by Functional Resources (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The SV-5a depicts the mapping of system functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. System Functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ac:title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term System Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.</p><p>The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A System Function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. System Functions can be carried out by Functional Resources (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b313a171-6d8b-432f-8526-8f596fcaf278" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="fc54f58e-9d3c-4bdc-9ec7-19d95fe07179" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="09cbc6d9-6d42-4ff3-b68f-d38b3baa9c2b" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Implements</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438842 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01211: (2026x) SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `254438842`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438842/2026x+SV-5b+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:19.564+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5b-operational-activity-to-systems-traceability-matrix', 'sv-5b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-5b addresses the linkage between described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='']. The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:

- Tracing system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Systems are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systems and activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='']). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Function
- Operational Activity
- Activity Performed By Performer
- Implements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Systems are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" ac:title="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systems and activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6463060-a272-4066-8e99-21ae169e71ff" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2fa31fbf-526a-4ca6-b632-047fb943c97c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a95b3a5c-79c0-4716-99ab-9d21042dff51" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Activity Performed By Performer</li><li>Implements</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438833 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01212: (2026x) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `254438833`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438833/2026x+SV-6+Systems+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:18.838+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-6-systems-resource-flow-matrix', 'sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-6 specifies the characteristics of the System Resource Flows exchanged between systems with emphasis on resources crossing the system boundary.

The SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system Resource Flow and the system Resource Flow content in a tabular format.

The intended usage of the SV-6 includes:

- Detailed definition of Resource Flows.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- A SV-6 table.
- An SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).
- A SV-6 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the Resource Flow exchanges specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SV-6 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type
- Function

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438796 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01213: (2026x) SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix

- page_id: `254438796`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438796/2026x+SV-7+Systems+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:14.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-7-systems-measures-matrix', 'sv-7', 'sv-7-actual-measures-table', 'sv-7-typical-measures-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in a SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- A SV-7 typical measures table. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-7_sys_typical_mea_matrix.png']
- A SV-7 actual measures table. [ATTACHMENT filename='SvcV-7_Actual.PNG']
- A SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report.

The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating SV-7 Actual table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Measure Type
- Measure
- Measurement
- Actual Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in a SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).</li><li>Identification of non-functional requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-7 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-7 typical measures table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="SV-7 Systems Typical Measures Matrix" ac:title="SV-7 Systems Typical Measures Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-7_sys_typical_mea_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A SV-7 actual measures table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="SV-7 Systems Actual Measures Matrix" ac:title="SV-7 Systems Actual Measures Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SvcV-7_Actual.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p>The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="e7c5c8ac-ab38-4bb0-bc00-a61e93a89c8e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SV-7 Typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating SV-7 Actual table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="858bd31e-8dd0-441c-be24-54e3014c0b26" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li><li>Measure Type</li><li>Measure</li><li>Measurement</li><li>Actual Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438851 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01214: (2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description

- page_id: `254438851`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438851/2026x+SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:20.555+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-8-systems-evolution-description', 'sv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-8 presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (systems), describing how they change over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

The intended usage of the SV-8 includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The SV-8 view can be represented using a SV-8 Systems Evolution Description diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-8 model shows the structure of resources modeled in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='']. The changes depicted in the SV-8 are derived from the project milestones that are shown in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space='']. When the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) PV-2 Project Timelines' space=''] is used for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two models.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Actual Project Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438870 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01215: (2026x) SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `254438870`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438870/2026x+SV-9+Systems+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:21.232+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-9-systems-technology-and-skills-forecast', 'sv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-9 defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills as well as the expected improvements or trends. New technologies and skills are tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''] milestones and linked to Capability Phases.

The SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the architecture.

The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant:

- Emerging capabilities.
- Industry trends.
- Predictions (with associated confidence factors) of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software systems.
- Current and possible future skills.

In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and systems, the DoDAF-described Model SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture. Given the future-oriented nature of this model, forecasts are typically made in short, mid and long-term timeframes, such as 6, 12 and 18-month intervals.

The intended usage of the SV-9 includes:

- Forecasting technology readiness against time.
- HR Trends Analysis.
- Recruitment Planning.
- Planning technology insertion.
- Input to options analysis.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-9 DoDAF-described Model forecasts relates to the Standards Profile ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='']) in that a timed forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a resource. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to the Standards Forecasts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='']) in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology or skill becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Protocol
- Standard

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438630 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01216: (2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description

- page_id: `254438630`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438630/2026x+SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:02.437+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-1-services-context-description', 'svcv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984445829

984445832

984445830

**Description**

The SvcV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of Services. For DoDAF V2.0, SvcV-1 incorporates human elements as types of Performers - Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

The intended usage of the SvcV-1 includes:

- Definition of service concepts.
- Definition of service options.
- Service Resource Flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- Service integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SvcV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SvcV-1 can be represented using a SvcV-1 diagram which is based on the UML class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-1 links together the operational and services architecture models by depicting how resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']. A SvcV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (i.e., in a “To-Be” Architectural Description), and so there may be many alternative SvcV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in annus-Is” Architectural Description, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SvcV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

Some Resources can carry out service functions (activities) as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] models and these functions can optionally be overlaid on a SvcV-1.

The SvcV-1 depicts all Resource Flows between resources that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between resources may be further specified in detail in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''] model and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space=''].

984445828

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438608 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01217: (2026x) SvcV-10a Services Rules Model

- page_id: `254438608`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438608/2026x+SvcV-10a+Services+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:05:57.329+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10a-services-rules-model', 'svcv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10a is to specify functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).

The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).

The intended usage of the SvcV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10a can be represented using:

- A SvcV-10a table.
- A SvcV-10a spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''], the SvcV-10a focuses physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Service Access

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

implementation aspects of the architecture (that is, the structural and behavioral elements of the Services Model).</p><p>The SvcV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Service Model.</p><p>The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (that is, non-functional).</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10a <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439381 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01218: (2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description

- page_id: `254439381`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439381/2026x+SvcV-10b+Services+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:02.488+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10b-services-state-transition-description', 'svcv-10b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. However, the relationship between the actions included in SvcV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='']. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- Service Access

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description. SvcV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the service functions. Alternatively, SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single service function, or the sequencing of service functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10b includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events, and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" ac:title="SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SvcV-10b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of service functions described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. However, the relationship between the actions included in <span class="confluence-link">SvcV-10b</span> and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the Architectural Description and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SvcV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c31ce39b-0068-4e61-a296-fe18c5d346bd" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resources State Machine</li><li>Resource State</li><li>Service Access</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438635 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01219: (2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `254438635`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438635/2026x+SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:02.558+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-10c-services-event-trace-description', 'svcv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description' space=''] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='']), Resource Flows (data, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='']) and entities (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='']) modeled in other models.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Event Trace
- Service Access
- Resource Role
- Resource Message
- Resource Operation

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SvcV-10c provides a time ordered examination of the interactions between services functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SvcV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of service functions and service data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or Service Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SvcV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SvcV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" ac:title="SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SvcV-10c.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SvcV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-10b Services State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SvcV-10c model may be related, in modeling terms, with Resource Flows (interactions, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /></ac:link>), Resource Flows (data, in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>) and entities (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a5d6f328-014d-44a5-aed1-2efecb665183" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="ed339d5b-fdbe-4c42-ae6d-1bee5672815c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="15ea3ba9-0d9a-44de-8fe2-4538e3eb6d31" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Event Trace</li><li>Service Access</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Resource Message</li><li>Resource Operation</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439434 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01220: (2026x) SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `254439434`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439434/2026x+SvcV-10c+Services+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:05.432+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SvcV-10c Services Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254438621 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01221: (2026x) SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `254438621`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254438621/2026x+SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:01.551+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > (2026x) Services viewpoint
- labels: ['svcv-2-services-resource-flow-description', 'svcv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984949823

984949826

984949825

**Description**

A SvcV-2 specifies the Resource Flows between Services and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SvcV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Services. This may be an existing connection or a specification of a connection that is to be made for a future connection.

The intended usage of the SvcV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SvcV-2 can be represented using:

- A SvcV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SvcV-2 Internal Block diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SvcV-2 diagram needs be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='']StdV-1 Standards Profile.

984949822
