## PAGE 01239: (2026x) Trustline

- page_id: `254439559`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439559/2026x+Trustline
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:20.511+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Elements > (2026x) UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1101099757

1101099759

1101099758

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that the trustingParty (either a Node or a KnownResource) trusts the trustedParty to a given level (indicated by the level attribute).

No unit of measure is associated with the level - security architects must define their own scale of trust levels for a given architecture or set of architectures.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| level : String | String denoting the level of Trust in the information source |

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254440006 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01250: (2026x) Using Expand/Collapse Internal Structure Compartment

- page_id: `254440006`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254440006/2026x+Using+Expand+Collapse+Internal+Structure+Compartment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:51.072+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['expand-internal-structure-compartment', 'collapse-internal-structure-compartment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Expand / Collapse internal structure compartment** functionality is available from the element’s smart manipulator toolbar.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you have an internal structure defined for the selected element, after clicking the Expand internal structure compartment button the compartment is expanded and the existing internal structure is displayed in it.

If you have more that one internal structure defined, none of them are displayed in the expanded compartment after clicking the **Expand internal structure compartment** button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Expand / Collapse internal structure compartment</strong> functionality is available from the element’s smart manipulator toolbar.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_internal_structure_compartment_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Expand/Collapse Internal Structure Compartment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>If you have an internal structure defined for the selected element, after clicking the Expand internal structure compartment button the compartment is expanded and the existing internal structure is displayed in it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have more that one internal structure defined, none of them are displayed in the expanded compartment after clicking the <strong>Expand internal structure compartment</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254440003 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01251: (2026x) Using Libraries

- page_id: `254440003`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254440003/2026x+Using+Libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:07:49.954+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['libraries', 'universal-joint-task-list', 'ujtl', 'dod-it-standards-registry', 'disr', 'joint-conditions-library', 'add-standard-policy-from-disr-library']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UPDM 2 plugin supports three element libraries:

1. The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).
2. DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).
3. Joint Conditions.

The **Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)**, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UPDM UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.

The **DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)** is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UPDM, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UPDM element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.

The **Joint Conditions Library** supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.

**Case Study**

Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.

To add a standard/policy from the DISR library

1. Create the StdV-1 diagram.
2. Create new or add an existing system element.
3. In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the Edit button. The Select Standard dialog opens.
4. Click the DISR Library tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.
5. Select the standard and do one of the following:
  - Double-click the selected standard to add it to the Selected elements list.
  - Click the + button to add the selected standard to the Selected elements list.
6. Click OK , after you finish selecting the standards.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>UPDM 2 plugin supports three element libraries:</p><ol><li>The Universal Joint Task List (UJTL).</li><li>DoD Information Technology Standards and Profile Registry (DISR).</li><li>Joint Conditions.</li></ol><p>The <strong>Universal Joint Task List (UJTL)</strong>, when augmented with the Service task lists, is a comprehensive integrated menu of functional tasks, conditions, measures, and criteria. It supports all levels of the Department of Defense in executing the National Military Strategy. In UPDM UJTL library consists of a list of Operational Activities representing universal joint tasks.</p><p>The <strong>DoD IT Standards Registry (DISR)</strong> is an online repository of IT standards formerly captured in the Joint Technical Architecture (JTA), Version 6.0. DISR replaces JTA. In UPDM, DISR is the list of standard elements that any of UPDM element may conform to. DISR Library usage is the same as UJTL library. Library item indexes are included when searching for elements within the element Selection dialog.</p><p>The <strong>Joint Conditions Library</strong> supports physical, military, and civil locations, where joint tasks can be performed.You can use it, when selecting Locations.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Case Study</strong></p><p>Let us say, we need to add a standard or a policy for a system element using the StdV-1 Standards Profile table. We will select a standard from the DISR library.</p><p>To add a standard/policy from the DISR library</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the StdV-1 diagram.</li><li>Create new or add an existing system element.</li><li>In the Standard / Policy cell of the created or added row, click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. The<strong> Select Standard</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>DISR Library</strong> tab. The list of DISR standards is displayed.</li><li>Select the standard and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Double-click the selected standard to add it to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button to add the selected standard to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, after you finish selecting the standards.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting standards from DISR Library" ac:title="Selecting standards from DISR Library"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_standard_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using Libraries" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439103 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01252: (2026x) Using SysML Compliance Mode

- page_id: `254439103`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439103/2026x+Using+SysML+Compliance+Mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:39.073+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['sysml-compliance-mode']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use SysML capabilities in your UPDM model. For that purpose you can enable the SysML compliance mode in one of the following ways:

- Enable the SysML compliance mode while creating a new project.
- Enable the SysML compliance mode after the project is created.

To enable SysML compliance mode while creating a new project

1. Click File > New Project . The New Project dialog opens.
2. Select project template, specify its name, and location.
3. In the <project template> Project Options area, set the SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level) property value to true .
4. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To turn on SysML compliance mode after the project is created

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Select General > UPDM .
3. In the **UPDM** property group, set the **SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level)** property value to *true*. You can use the Quick filter box to find the **SysML compliance mode (UPDM L1 compliance level)** property more quickly. Simply start typing the property name in the Quick filter box.
4. Click OK .

When the SysML compliance mode is turned on, control flows are represented as dashed line in activity based diagrams. You can change the representation style to the solid line.

To change the representation to the solid line

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Select General > UPDM .
3. In the **UPDM** properties group, set the **Display solid control flows in SysML compliance mode** property value to *true*. You can use the Quick filter box to find the **Display solid control flows in SysML compliance mode** property more quickly. Simply start typing the property name in the Quick filter box.
4. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case Study**

The following example describes the actual situation, how you can use the SysML compliance mode in the UPDM model.

Let us say, we need to describe a full-time equivalent (FTE) calculations in the UPDM model.

First, we need to create the typical organizational structure using OV-4 Organizational Relationship Chart diagram. The typical organizational structure defines types of departments and roles and how they are associated together in the modeled enterprise. The Department can contain any number of Employees and any number of sub Departments.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Secondly, we generate an actual organizational chart based on the typical organizational chart (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Instantiating Structures' space='']). An actual organizational chart shows concrete departments and residing employees.

Each employee works a certain number of FTEs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Thirdly, we can use the SysML Parametric Diagram to describe the recursive FTE’s calculation for the selected department and each residing sub-department.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can execute the described calculations using Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For that purpose you can enable the SysML compliance mode in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Enable the SysML compliance mode while creating a new project.</li><li>Enable the SysML compliance mode after the project is created.</li></ul><p>To enable SysML compliance mode while creating a new project</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>New Project</strong>. Simply start typing the property name in the Quick filter box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>When the SysML compliance mode is turned on, control flows are represented as dashed line in activity based diagrams. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439110 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01253: (2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin

- page_id: `254439110`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439110/2026x+Using+SysML+elements+in+UPDM+2+plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:39.234+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Using SysML Compliance Mode
- labels: ['expand-sysml-toolbars', 'use-sysml-elements-in-updm-2-plugin']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SysML elements can be used to extend UPDM provided set of concepts.

SysML toolbars are available in the following diagrams:

| Icon | View |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  | StV-1 |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |

SysML toolbars are collapsed by default.

To expand SysML toolbars

1. Click Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog, click the UPDM property group.
3. Set the System Engineer User Interface Mode property value to true .
4. Click OK .

****

**Case Study**

We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.

Do the following

1. Create an OV-2 diagram.
2. Create a Performer/ Node in the diagram.
3. On the SysML Requirements Diagram toolbar, click the Requirement button.
4. Create a Requirement in the diagram pane.
5. Create the Satisfy relationship between the Performer/ Node and the Requirement.

As you can see in the following figure, we have expressed that the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) CV-1 Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="47"><ri:attachment ri:filename="StV-1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">StV-1</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="47"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="47"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="47"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P1_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>SysML toolbars are collapsed by default.</p><p>To expand SysML toolbars</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, click the <strong>UPDM</strong> property group.</li><li>Set the <strong>System Engineer User Interface Mode</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Case Study</strong></p><p>We will use the Requirement element, which is a SysML element, in an OV-2 diagram.</p><p>Do the following</p><hr /><ol><li>Create an OV-2 diagram.</li><li>Create a Performer/ Node in the diagram.</li><li>On the <strong>SysML Requirements Diagram</strong> toolbar, click the <strong>Requirement</strong> button.</li><li>Create a Requirement in the diagram pane.</li><li>Create the Satisfy relationship between the Performer/ Node and the Requirement.</li></ol><p>As you can see in the following figure, we have expressed that the architecture element satisfies the Requirement.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_SysML.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using SysML elements in UPDM 2 plugin" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917121858

917121860

917121859

##### Description

An end that describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved; a mental image of what the future will or could be like.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| statement : VisionStatement[*] | A description of the Vision. |
| enterprisePhase : EnterprisePhase[1] | The phase which temporally locates the Vision. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF Views

[CV-1 Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/(2026x) (2026x) CV-1+Vision)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254439145 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01259: (2026x) Working With Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `254439145`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/254439145/2026x+Working+With+Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:06:40.450+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Coverage Analysis
- labels: ['create-metric-table', 'show-metric-suites', 'hide-metric-suites', 'calculate-new-metric', 'recalculate', 'add-new-metric-with-different-parameters', 'show-abbreviation-in-column-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.

To create a metric table

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a package which will be a metric parameter.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_metric_table.png']
3. Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='metric_table.png']

The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the **Options** button and select the **Show Abbreviation in Column Name**option.

When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.

To show or hide metric suites in the metric table

1. In the metric table toolbar, click the Show Metric Suites button.
2. Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.

There are three actions with the metrics. You can:

- Calculate New Metric . Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.
- Recalculate . Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.
- Add New Metric with Different Parameters . Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.</p><p>To create a metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a package which will be a metric parameter.</li><li><p>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New Metric Table</strong>.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_metric_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working With Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working With Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><p> </p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="bbc2d883-7fa9-4baa-8fbe-705bc7349248" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Options</strong> button and select the <strong>Show Abbreviation in Column Name </strong>option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.</p><p>To show or hide metric suites in the metric table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the metric table toolbar, click the <strong>Show Metric Suites</strong> button.</li><li>Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.</li></ol><p>There are three actions with the metrics. You can:</p><ul><li><strong>Calculate New Metric</strong>. Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.</li><li><strong>Recalculate</strong>. Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.</li><li><strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong>. Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575708 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01261: A1 Meta-Data Definitions

- page_id: `249575708`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575708/A1+Meta-Data+Definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T07:15:29.174+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a1-meta-data-definitions', 'a1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The A1 Meta-Data Definitions view specifies the categories of meta-data tag used throughout the architecture.

Architectures, particularly large ones, usually require meta-data tags to aid with searching and discovery. NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:

- Definition.
- Assumption.
- Finding.
- Recommendation.
- Purpose.
- Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.

**Implementation**

A1 can be represented using :

- An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='A1.png']
- An A1 MS Word document report.
- A DARS report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report in A1' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

NAF specifies some built-in meta-data tags, such as:</p><ul><li>Definition.</li><li>Assumption.</li><li>Finding.</li><li>Recommendation.</li><li>Purpose.</li><li>Approver, Approval Milestone, Modeller, Manager, Responsible Owner, Tool Used.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>A1 can be represented using :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4cfe41a3-120d-4bdc-90d5-0c3ae656868d">An A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:image ac:alt="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram" ac:title="A1 Meta-Data Definitions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="A1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="A1 Meta-Data Definitions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An A1 MS Word document report.</li><li>A DARS report.<br /><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in A1" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Architectural Description</li><li>Defines Architecture</li><li>Architecture Metadata</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01265: A8 Standards

- page_id: `249575716`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575716/A8+Standards
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-01-08T07:11:34.993+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint
- labels: ['a8-standards', 'a8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A8 Standards table describes the current standards of the architecture and the systems elements that should conform to them. A8 Standards Forecast table provides a description of emerging standards and their potential impact on current systems and services view elements within a set of time frames within the architecture.

**Implementation**

A8 can be represented using:

- An A8 Standards table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_standards_table.png']
- An A8 Standards Forecast table. [ATTACHMENT filename='A8_forecast.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

## PAGE 01269: Actual Location

- page_id: `249575746`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575746/Actual+Location
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:47.138+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: UPDM Extension

1097442609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: UPDM Extension

1097442611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: UPDM Extension

1097442610

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalLocation (MODAF::ActualLocation, NAF::ActualLocation) is a location anywhere on the earth. The means of describing the location is a string (locationDescription). The information contained in that string is governed by the taxonomy reference - e.g. if the PhysicalLocation is a “GPS reference”, the string will contain the GPS coordinates.

#### INFO: UPDM Extension

UPDM Extension

This has been extended in UPDM to include non-earth locations.

DoDAF: All subtypes of «IndividualType» Location, such as Facility, Site, etc.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An actual specific organization, an instance of an organization class, for example, “The US Department of Defense”.

DoDAF: [DoDAF::Organization]: A specific real-world assemblage of people and other resources organized for an on-going purpose.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ratifiedStandards : Standard[*] | The standards that were ratified by the ActualOrganization. |

## PAGE 01276: Actual Project

- page_id: `249575781`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575781/Actual+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:19:46.621+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337141382

1337141384

1337141383

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project): A time-limited endeavour to create a specific set of products or services.

DoDAF: (DoDAF::Project): A temporary endeavor undertaken to create Resources or Desired Effects.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| endDate : ISO8601DateTime[0..1] | An end time of a Project. |
| startDate :ISO8601DateTime[1] | A start time for a Project. |
| part : Project[0..*] | The sub-projects. |
| whole : Project[0..1] | A parent project. |
| ownedMilestones : ActualProjectMilestone[1..*] | The milestones associated with a project. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01277: Actual Project Milestone

- page_id: `249575782`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575782/Actual+Project+Milestone
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:02:31.773+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Project Viewpoint and Acquisition Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337194352

1337194354

1337194353

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: (ProjectMilestone): An event in a ActualProject (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project) by which progress is measured.

In the case of an acquisition project, there are two key types of milestones which shall be represented using subtypes - IncrementMilestone (MODAF::CapabilityIncrement, NAF::CapabilityIncrement) and OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService)

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| date : ISO8601DateTime[1] | Defines time for a ProjectMilestone. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48dd401d-c6f2-4c3a-902f-6605296ffbf0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1337194352</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a51f464-db0c-4da9-a6bb-c10f91f1f3ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1337194354</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="068d88ae-cc81-4db7-8d91-50c822948466"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1337194353</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: (ProjectMilestone): An event in a ActualProject (MODAF::Project, NAF::Project) by which progress is measured.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae6e9325-6760-4096-8a86-041dd6d35330"><ac:rich-text-body>In the case of an acquisition project, there are two key types of milestones which shall be represented using subtypes - IncrementMilestone (MODAF::CapabilityIncrement, NAF::CapabilityIncrement) and OutOfServiceMilestone (MODAF::OutOfService, NAF::OutOfService)</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>DoDAF: NA</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, DoDAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Properties</span></h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>date : ISO8601DateTime[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Defines time for a ProjectMilestone.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Instance Specification</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

- version_date: `2015-08-10T08:27:55.686+02:00`

- labels: ['actual-project-milestone-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.

To open the **Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard**

1. Open a AcV-2 (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)' space='UPDM2P'] (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P'] (DoDAF 2.0).
2. Select a project.
3. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
4. Select one of the available milestone kinds: The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Milestone name, kind, and date specification. How to specify a name, kind, and date of a Milestone?In this step, you can specify a name of a new Milestone you are creating.You can select one of the Milestone kinds, which are:Out Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneDeployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneActual Project MilestoneAlso you can set the Milestone date and time. Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P'].[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Project milestone type specification. How to specify a milestone type?You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:DLOD MilestoneDOTMLPF MilestoneYou can create your own milestone type as well.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Project status specification at a particular Milestone. How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.DLOD Milestone typeDOTMLPF Milestone typeProject threadsEquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTrainingDoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilitiesStatus indicatorsDLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical IssuesMateriel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & SupportYou can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.To create project threads and status indicators1. In the Project Thread area, click the **Add** button.2. Specify the thread name.3. In the Status Indicator area, click the **Add** button.4. Specify the indicator name.5. Click the **Fill Color** button.6. In the **Color** dialog select the color and click **OK**.To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone** button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Milestones in Lr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> is used to define a new milestone for the selected project.</p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a AcV-2 (MODAF),<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Lr Lines of Development]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF 4.0), NPV-1 (NAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="PV-2 Project Timelines" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF 2.0).</li><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><ol><li><p>Milestone name, kind, and date specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cfba52f0-5924-4171-bb25-13e507a03da1" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a  name, kind, and date of a Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step, you can specify a name of a new Milestone you are creating.</p><p>You can select one of the Milestone kinds, which are:</p><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul><p> </p><p>Also you can set the Milestone date and time. Date and time indicates the position of the milestone in the project time frame. For more information on setting date ant time, see<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying milestone name, kind, and date in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project milestone type specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6f2e06c-d94e-4e52-9132-2028c17d803a" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a milestone type?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select one (or both) of the given milestone types:</p><ul><li>DLOD Milestone</li><li>DOTMLPF Milestone</li></ul><p>You can create your own milestone type as well.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Selecting Actual Project Milestone Type in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Project status specification at a particular Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="c969062e-7f9b-45eb-9945-e8af5d9cb358" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a project status of a particular Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating a new milestone, you can apply the project status according to the selected milestone type. Each milestone type can have specific project threads and status indicators.</p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>DLOD Milestone type</th><th>DOTMLPF Milestone type</th></tr><tr><td>Project threads</td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Status indicators</td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You can also define you own milestone type with a custom set of project threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create project threads and status indicators</p><hr /><p>1. In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>2. Specify the thread name.</p><p>3. In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>4. Specify the indicator name.</p><p>5. Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</p><p>6. In the <strong>Color</strong> dialog select the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="80994452-914a-4976-ad03-8607d73c10a0" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators are created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" ac:title="Specifying Project status at a particular Milestone in Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_project_milestone_creation_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9679fdea-7166-452c-b060-b6b9497a4189" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Adding Milestones in Lr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Adding Milestones in PV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575566 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01282: Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `249575566`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575566/Adding+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:04:59.583+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-capability-in-cr', 'add-capability-provision-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Dr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575114 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01283: Adding Capabilities in CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `249575114`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575114/Adding+Capabilities+in+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:38:18.687+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['add-capability-in-cv-3', 'add-capability-provision-in-cv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414728880

1414728882

1414728881

**To add a new Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability .
2. Select the Capability owner in the Select Owner dialog. An empty line for the newly created capability appears on the diagram pane.

**To add an existing Capability**

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

**To add a new Capability Provision**

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, choose Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

- page_id: `249576058`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576058/Adding+custom+columns+in+tables

- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Adding custom columns in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575614 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01285: Adding Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `249575614`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575614/Adding+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-20T15:47:43.472+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575615 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01287: Adding Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `249575615`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575615/Adding+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:28:10.492+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Project.

To add an existing Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Project dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Sub Project

1. Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Project .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project , choose Add new Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select a Project (or several Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Project for which you want to create a Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Sub Project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575567 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01289: Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram

- page_id: `249575567`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575567/Adding+Sub+Capabilities+in+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:05:11.712+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['add-sub-capability-in-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']or already [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']Sub Capability to Cr diagram.

newTo add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

existingTo add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A UPDM Artifact used to define an alternative name for an element as used by DoDAF, MODAF, or NAF.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| --- | --- |
| nameOwner : String[*] | The person or organization that uses an alternative name. |

566188275

566188274

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

566188271

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576043 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01293: Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `249576043`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576043/Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-09T13:35:02.563+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Actual Measurements can be applied to measurable elements only. 
 
 To make an element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Types property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser to the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set and you will be able to use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button  to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576014 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01294: Applying military symbols

- page_id: `249576014`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576014/Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-20T10:57:15.507+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P'] views.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Symbol dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
  - Open the Specification window of any UPDM element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for resource shape.

- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:15:25.536+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['apply-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-project-status-in-pv-2', 'edit-status-in-pv-2', 'apply-status-in-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

31012031

31012034

31012032

To apply the Project Status to a new Milestone

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the Project status by following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P'] .

To edit the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the project status following the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P']. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Specify Project Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

31012030

**Related Pages:**

**true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P']**

1096137270

1096137269

##### Description

UPDM: Represents a visual indication of a connection used in high level operational concept diagrams. The connections are purely visual and cannot be related to any architectural semantics.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/MODAF.+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575730 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01298: Architectural Description

- page_id: `249575730`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575730/Architectural+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:11:11.677+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1095585534

1095585537

1095585536

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A specification of a system of systems at a technical level which also provides the business context for the system of systems.

DoDAF: Information describing architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| approvalAuthority: ActualOrganizationalResource[*] | References the actual organizational resource that has the authority to approve the architectural description. |
| architect : String[*] | The name of the architect responsible for the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| assumptionAndConstraint : String[*] | Any assumptions, constraints, and limitations contained in the ArchitecturalDescription, including those affecting deployment, communications performance, information assurance environments, etc. |
| creatingOrganization : String[1] | Describes the ActualOrganizationalResource creating the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| dateCompleted : String[0..1] | Date that the Architectural Description was completed. |
| purpose : String[1] | Explains the need for the Architecture, what it will demonstrate, the types of analyses that will be applied to it, who is expected to perform the analyses, what decisions are expected to be made on the basis of each form of analysis, who is expected to make those decisions, and what actions are expected to result. |
| recommendations : String[1] | States the recommendations that have been developed based on the architecture effort. Examples include recommended system implementations, and opportunities for technology insertion. |
| summaryOfFindings : String[1] | Summarizes the findings that have been developed so far. This may be updated several times during the development of the ArchitecturalDescription. |
| toolsUsed : String[1] | Identifies any tools used to develop the ArchitecturalDescription as well as the file names and formats if appropriate. |
| toBe : Boolean[1] | Indicates whether the ArchitecturalDescription is an existing or future one. |
| Viewpoint : String[1] |  |
| Views : View[1..*] |  |
| architectureFramework : Architecture- FrameworkKind [1] | The architectureFramework tag identifies the subset of aliases to use within the context of the ArchitecturalDescription (i. e. DoDAF, MODAF or none) |

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UPDM2P']

##### Related MODAF views

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UPDM2P190']

##### Related NAF views

[NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ArchitecturalDescription, including those affecting deployment, communications performance, information assurance environments, etc.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>creatingOrganization : String[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Describes the ActualOrganizationalResource creating the ArchitecturalDescription.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>dateCompleted : String[0..1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Date that the Architectural Description was completed.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>purpose : String[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Explains the need for the Architecture, what it will demonstrate, the types of analyses that will be applied to it, who is expected to perform the analyses, what decisions are expected to be made on the basis of each form of analysis, who is expected to make those decisions, and what actions are expected to result.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>recommendations : String[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>States the recommendations that have been developed based on the architecture effort. Examples include recommended system implementations, and opportunities for technology insertion.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>summaryOfFindings : String[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Summarizes the findings that have been developed so far. This may be updated several times during the development of the ArchitecturalDescription.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>toolsUsed : String[1]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Identifies any tools used to develop the ArchitecturalDescription as well as the file names and formats if appropriate.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><span>toBe : Boolean[1]</span></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Indicates whether the ArchitecturalDescription is an existing or future one.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><span>Viewpoint : String[1]</span></p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><span>Views : View[1..*]</span></p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><span>architectureFramework : Architecture- FrameworkKind [1]</span></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><span>The architectureFramework tag identifies the subset of aliases to use within the context of the ArchitecturalDescription (i. e. DoDAF, MODAF or none)</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Extensions</h3><p><span>UML Package</span></p><h3><span>Related DoDAF views</span></h3><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ac:link></p><h3><span>Related MODAF views</span></h3><p><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ac:link><br /></span></p><h3><span>Related NAF views</span></h3><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information">NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information</a></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575707 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01300: Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `249575707`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575707/Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:13:55.331+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575915 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01303: Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `249575915`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575915/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T08:30:59.911+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UPDM architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UPDM elements and right-click them.
2. On the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

1. Selecting parts. How to select a part?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.
2. Selecting a package. How to select a package?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.
3. Creating a diagram. How to create a diagram?[IMAGE alt='' src='']In this step, specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating Structures' space='UPDM2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575098 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01304: AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `249575098`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575098/AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-08-07T19:36:38.721+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

596919250

596919253

596919251

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview & Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

596919249

**Related Pages:**

- Architectural Description
- Architectural Reference
- Architecture Metadata
- Generating document based reports
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating DARS report in AV-1' space='UPDM2P']

The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.</p><p>The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:</p><ul><li>In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.</li><li>When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.</li></ul><p>The AV-1 may be used to:</p><ul><li>Scope the architecture effort.</li><li>Provide context to the architecture effort.</li><li>Define the architecture effort.</li><li>Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.</li><li>Assist search within an architecture repository.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>Using the following (.docx) reports:<br /><p>- AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information.</p><p>- AV-1 DARS.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.</p><p>The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45f4a4d0-e187-4313-bbb1-c8fc256f896c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">596919249</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Architectural+Description">Architectural Description</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Architectural+Reference">Architectural Reference</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Architecture+Metadata">Architecture Metadata</a></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Generating+document+based+reports">Generating document based reports</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating DARS report in AV-1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template in AV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
