<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575102 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01305: AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `249575102`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575102/AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-08-10T01:46:14.339+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

650486003

650486006

650486005

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

650486002

**Related Pages:**

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9559e431-445d-4c34-9426-51db334eda69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe407845-09fa-484a-b0eb-8e51e2284418"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486006</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b82d306-b81c-4c55-b59d-840ae3d9c959"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486005</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).</p><p>An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.</p><p>It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title=" AV-2 table" ac:alt=" AV-2 table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AV-2_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af85c59-bc97-46a5-929b-e35704b36d7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">650486002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Definition">Definition</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Alias">Alias</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Same+As">Same As</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575542 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01306: C1 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `249575542`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575542/C1+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:29:36.072+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c1-capability-taxonomy', 'c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C1 Capability Taxonomy view covers capabilities and the hierarchical relationships between them. It specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures – i.e. one C1 may provide the definitive list of capabilities for a number of logical and resource architectures. In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P'], and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.

The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). A capability taxonomy persists over time (an architect may wish to specify historical, current or future capability) and may be referenced by multiple architectures.

In NAF, a capability is a description of an ability to do something. In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. A C1 is most commonly structured as a specialization hierarchy of capabilities, with the most general at the root and most specific at the leaves. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C3 Capability Dependency' space='UPDM2P'] is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.

The C1 view may be used to:

- Identify an existing and required capabilities.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.
- Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.

**Implementation**

The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C1 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In NAF, Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of one or more enterprises. This means that it is feasible to develop a capability taxonomy that will apply to all Enterprise Phases listed in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and an C1 may even cover more than one logical / resource architecture.</p><p>The capabilities may be organized into specialization hierarchies (taxonomies). In C1, the capabilities are only described in the abstract – i.e. C1 does not specify how a capability is to be implemented. Measures of effectiveness may be specified for each capability. Note that measures of effectiveness are inherited down a capability taxonomy.</p><p class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C3 Capability Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> is the view that describes dependencies between the capabilities shown in the C1 taxonomy.</p><p>The C1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify an existing and required capabilities.</li><li><span>Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of KURs.</span></li><li>Provide a reference capabilities for multiple architectures.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C1 view can be represented using a C1 Capability Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C1 Capability Taxonomy" ac:title="C1 Capability Taxonomy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="c1_capability_taxonomy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Climate</li><li>Environment</li><li>Environment Property</li><li>Light Condition</li><li>Location</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575545 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01307: C2 Enterprise Vision

- page_id: `249575545`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575545/C2+Enterprise+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:29:51.989+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c2-enterprise-vision', 'c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C2 Enterprise Vision view provides the high-level scope for the architecture and a strategic context for the capabilities the architecture contains. In essence it describes the end-state for any business transformation activity. The purpose of an C2 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the architecture and to specify the scope for the architecture. The C2 describes the vision, goals, enduring tasks and capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers.

The Enterprise Goals and Capabilities are ‘enduring’, that is they are intended to provide an enduring framework across the lifetime of the enterprise that is being modeled. An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.

The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Scenario' space='UPDM2P'], is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. P1 & D2are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.

The C2 view may be used to:

- Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.
- Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.
- Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).
- Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.

It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entitiesstereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C2 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An Enterprise is essentially time-bounded: it has a start and end date. It is broken into a set of phases for capability planning purposes. The time periods associated with these phases are sometimes referred to as ‘epochs’ by military planners although NAF does not use this specific terminology. It is sometimes necessary in NAF to identify one Enterprise Phase as being that represents the ‘As Is’ capability of the Enterprise. The Enterprise Goals relate to the entire enterprise lifetime, they are not specific to an Enterprise Phase. Each goal may optionally include attributes associated with the measurable outcomes that relate to it.</p><p>The C2 only shows the capabilities exhibited by enterprises. It does not show how the enterprise is structured in order to deliver those capabilities – <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Scenario" /></ac:link>, is used to define the logical structure of the enterprise, and the individual logical nodes in the enterprise that deliver the capability. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">P1 &amp; D2 </span>are used to show the physical and organizational structure of the enterprise.</p><p>The C2 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Capture and communication of the strategic vision related to capability evolution.</li><li>Identify the capabilities required to meet the vision and goals.</li><li>Identify the required timescales for the capabilities (as opposed to Cr which provides a summary of when projects are estimated to deliver capability).</li><li>Identify any enduring tasks the enterprise performs.</li></ul><p>It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C2 view can be represented using a C2 Enterprise Vision diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="C2 Enterprise Vision" ac:title="C2 Enterprise Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575549 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01308: C3 Capability Dependency

- page_id: `249575549`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575549/C3+Capability+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:30:05.858+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c3-capability-dependency', 'c3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C3 Capability Dependency view describes the dependencies between capabilities. It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.

The C3 view contrasts with [C1](http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy)which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).

The C3 view may be used to:

- Identify capability dependencies.
- Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).

**Implementation**

The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C3 diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It also defines logical composition of capabilities (i.e.capability clusters). The C3 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities and between capability clusters. The composition of capabilities (into clusters) is logical, and the purpose of the clusters is to guide enterprise management.</p><p>The C3 view contrasts with <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/UPDM182/C1+Capability+Taxonomy">C1 </a>which only addresses specialization and composition relationships (i.e. capability taxonomy).</p><p>The C3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Identify capability dependencies.</li><li>Manage capability (impact analysis for options, disposal etc).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C3 view can be represented using a C3 Capability Dependency diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:height="552" ac:title=" C3 Capability Dependency" ac:width="542"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C3 Capability Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C3 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575552 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01309: C4 Standard Processes

- page_id: `249575552`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575552/C4+Standard+Processes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:37:59.641+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c4-standard-processes', 'c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C4 Standard Processes view specifies Standard Operational Activities, and optionally traces them to the capabilities they support. Some processes are standard across the whole enterprise, or even more than one enterprise. The Standard Operational Activities can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P']).

The C4 view may be used to:

- Specify a doctrine.
- Trace capabilities to enduring tasks.
- Trace capabilities to standard operational activities.
- Audit a capability.

**Implementation**

The C4 view can be represented using a C4 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Standard Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C4 matrix' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Standard Operational Activities can be re-used across multiple logical architectures (e.g. in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The C4 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Specify a doctrine.</li><li>Trace capabilities to enduring tasks.</li><li>Trace capabilities to standard operational activities.</li><li>Audit a capability.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C4 view can be represented using a C4 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575558 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01311: C7 Performance Parameters

- page_id: `249575558`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575558/C7+Performance+Parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:38:32.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['c7-performance-parameters', 'c7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measure apply to each Capability.

The C7 view may be used to:

- Set a Capability Requirements.
- Military Estimates.
- Strategic Reviews.
- Plan an Assumptions.

**Implementation**

The C7 view can be represented using:

- C7 Actual Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- C7 Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C7 typical table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C7 Actual table' space='UPDM2P']Creating C7 Actual table

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Measurement Set
- Measurement

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.</p><p>The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measure apply to each Capability.</p><p>The C7 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Set a Capability Requirements.</li><li>Military Estimates.</li><li>Strategic Reviews.</li><li>Plan an Assumptions.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The C7 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">C7 Actual Performance Parameters <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">table.</span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image ac:alt="C7 Actual Performance Parameters" ac:title="C7 Actual Performance Parameters"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C7_actual.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">C7 Performance Parameters table.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><ac:image ac:alt="C7 Performance Parameters" ac:title="C7 Performance Parameters"><ri:attachment ri:filename="C7_typical.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C7 typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C7 Actual table" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Creating C7 Actual table</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Capability</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Measurement Set</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Measurement </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915142784

915142786

915142785

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A high level specification of the enterprise's ability.

DoDAF: The ability to achieve a desired effect under specified [performance] standards and conditions through combinations of ways and means [activities and resources] to perform a set of activities.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| environmentConditions : Environment[0..*] | The environmental conditions pertinent to this Capability typically including weather, threat, security, terrain, etc. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 01313: Capability Configuration

- page_id: `249575867`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575867/Capability+Configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:32.515+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Systems Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314878458

314878461

314878459

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise. A CapabilityConfiguration is a set of artifacts or an organization configured to provide a capability, and should be guided by [doctrine] which may take the form of Standard or OperationalConstraint stereotypes.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| doctrine : Constraint[1..*] | Represents the doctrinal line of the capability development. |
| deployedMilestone : ConfigurationDeployed[*] | The milestone at which the configuration is deployed. |
| noLongerUsedMilestone : ConfigurationNoLongerUsed[0..1] | The milestone at which the configuration is no longer used. |
| incrementMilestone : CapabilityIncrementMilestone[*] | The milestone for incrementing a CapabilityConfiguration. |
| outOfServiceMilestone : OutOfServiceMilestone[0..1] | The milestone at which the capability is out of service. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-3 Capability Phasing](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/CV-3+Capability+Phasing)

## PAGE 01314: Capability of Performer

- page_id: `249575771`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

916399279

916399282

916399281

##### Description

UPDM:A couple that represents the capability that a resource, node or enterprise phase exhibits (Exhibits).

MODAF/NAF: An assertion that a Node is required to have a Capability (Capability for node).

DoDAF: A couple that represents the capability that a performer has.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

[SvcV-1 Services Context Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/SvcV-1+Services+Context+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575919 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01316: Capability Provision Creation Wizard

- page_id: `249575919`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575919/Capability+Provision+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T09:29:39.336+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['capability-provision-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Provision Creation Wizard** is used to define a Capability Provision by one or more Resources at ta specific period of time.

To open the **Capability Provision Creation Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UPDM2P'] (DoDAF), StV-3 (MODAF), NCV-3 (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UPDM2P'] (NAF 4.0)
2. Select a Capability.
3. Do one of the following: The Capability Provision Creation Wizard opens.
  - On the <viewpoint name> diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - Right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .

The wizard consists of three steps:

1. Resources selection. How to select the existing or create new resources?In this step, you can select the existing or create new resources.For more information on selecting elements, see **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**, Step #2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Capability increment date specification. How to specify a the date and time?In this step, you can set the date and time, when the resource should begin provisioning the capability. The increment milestone is created.For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Out of service date specification (Optional). How to specify out of service date?In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The out of service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the capability exhibition bar on the chart will have the triangle ending.For more information on setting date ant time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- Adding Capabilities to CV-3 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575104 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01317: Capability viewpoint

- page_id: `249575104`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575104/Capability+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:28:23.480+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['capability-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845143062

845143064

845143063

“The Capability Viewpoint and the DoDAF-described Models within the viewpoint are introduced into DoDAF V2.0 to address the concerns of Capability Portfolio Managers. In particular, the Capability Models describe capability taxonomy and capability evolution.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

“The DoD increasingly employs incremental acquisition to help manage the risks of complex procurements. Consequently, there is a need to provide visualizations of the evolving capabilities so that Portfolio Managers can synchronize the introduction of capability increments across a portfolio of projects. The Capability Models included within DoDAF are based on the program and capability information used by Portfolio Managers to capture the increasingly complex relationships between interdependent projects and capabilities.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

800700

800700

845143061

**Related Pages:**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575541 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01322: Concepts viewpoint

- page_id: `249575541`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575541/Concepts+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:10:20.943+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['concepts-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concepts viewpoint supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The Concepts viewpoint further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision, concepts, NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Concepts viewpoint</span> further details the dependencies between capabilities, enabling capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

- space_key: `UPDM2P`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096512645

1096512647

1096512646

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A definition of the conditions in which something exists or functions. An Environment may be specified in terms of LocationType (e.g. terrain), Climate (e.g. tropical), and LightCondition (e.g. dark, light, dusk, etc.).

DoDAF: An object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Data Type

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A type of ResourceInteraction where one Resource (source) controls another (target). For example, the driver of a tank, one organization having operational control of another, a fire control system controlling a weapons system.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Information Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576010 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01326: Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks

- page_id: `249576010`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576010/Converting+Model+between+Enterprise+Architecture+Frameworks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T11:50:10.765+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['convert-to', 'convert-model-between-enterprise-architecture-frameworks']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)

1. Do either:
  - Change the perspective to another EAF's perspective.
  - Click **File** > **Convert****To** and select an EAF.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Question dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Question** dialog, specify the model conversion options.
3. Click Yes .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span> Do either:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Change the perspective to another EAF's perspective. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert</strong><strong> To</strong> and select an EAF.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Question dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Question</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, specify the model conversion options.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" ac:title="Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks"><ri:attachment ri:filename="convert_to_command.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576096 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01327: Coverage Analysis

- page_id: `249576096`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576096/Coverage+Analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T14:19:06.575+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['coverage-analysis', 'metrics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.

Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also you can create your own metrics suites from a scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.</p><p>Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help tracking the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575563 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01328: Cr Capability Roadmap

- page_id: `249575563`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575563/Cr+Capability+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T07:52:08.427+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['cr-capability-roadmap', 'cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Cr Capability Roadmap view provides a representation of the actual or estimated availability of capabilities over a period of time (derived from capability delivery milestones in acquisition projects). The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The Cr view indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P'] view specifies the requirement for capability at each Enterprise Phase, whereas the Cr view reflects the output of capability programmes (e.g. acquisition, training, etc.) over time. The requirement specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P']may or may not be met by those programmes.**

The Cr view may be used to:

- Plan Capability (capability phasing).
- Plan Capability integration.
- Analyze Capability gap.
- Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.

**Implementation**

The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying all Sub Capabilities in Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Sub Capabilities from Cr diagram' space='UPDM2P']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575720 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01329: Creating A8 Standards Forecast table

- page_id: `249575720`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575720/Creating+A8+Standards+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:43:22.090+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards Forecast table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards Forecast table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f1570457-54e3-4d88-a1ad-2a875582754a" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575719 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01330: Creating A8 Standards table

- page_id: `249575719`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575719/Creating+A8+Standards+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T16:37:24.041+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint > A8 Standards
- labels: ['create-a8-standards-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an A8 Standards table, do either

- Add a new element by clicking the Add New button. In the Create element wizard select element and its owner.
- Add the existing elements by clicking the Add Existing button and in the Select UPDM element dialog select the elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the **…** button on any cell in the **Standard/Policy** column.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an A8 Standards table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new element by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. In the <strong>Create element</strong> wizard select element and its owner.</li><li>Add the existing elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select UPDM element</strong> dialog select the elements.</li><li><p>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576005 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01331: Creating Blank UPDM Project

- page_id: `249576005`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576005/Creating+Blank+UPDM+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T11:29:05.044+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['new-project', 'create-blank-updm-project']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the blank UPDM project. In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. 
 
 To create a blank project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctrl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Enterprise Modeling**, select **Blank****UPDM****Project**.
3. Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the **General** property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.

You can always change the Architectural Framework in the **Project Options** dialog **General project options** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In that project, there will be no predefined structure, so you will be able to make your own. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To create a blank project</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Do one of the following:<br /></span></span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Select <strong>File</strong>&gt;<strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Press Ctrl+N.</span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li class="_mce_tagged_br"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">New</strong><strong> Project</strong> dialog, under <strong>Enterprise Modeling</strong>, select <strong>Blank </strong><strong>UPDM</strong><strong> Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</span></li></ol><p>By default, DoDAF 2.0 Architectural Framework will be selected in the <strong>General</strong> property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can always change the Architectural Framework in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog <strong>General project options</strong> group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:alt="New Project dialog" ac:title="New Project dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating Blank UPDM Project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(167,165,165);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575544 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01332: Creating C1 diagram

- page_id: `249575544`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575544/Creating+C1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-12T09:49:53.267+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C1 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575627 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01333: Creating C1-S1 matrix

- page_id: `249575627`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575627/Creating+C1-S1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T08:43:51.256+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1-s1-matrix', 'build-c1-s1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.

To create C1-S1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of this matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p>To create C1-S1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575547 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01334: Creating C2 diagram

- page_id: `249575547`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575547/Creating+C2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T13:51:32.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C2 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['create-c2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C2 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. Specify the types of parts. The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.

Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To complete fully the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P'], the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking <ac:image ac:alt="Create Element" ac:title="Create Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating C2 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator.</li><li><p>Specify the types of parts.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To complete fully the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

To create a C3 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575554 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01336: Creating C4 matrix

- page_id: `249575554`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575554/Creating+C4+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:00:12.895+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C4 Standard Processes
- labels: ['create-c4', 'build-c4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.

To create a C4 matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Standard Operational Activities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Standard Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a C4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575557 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01337: Creating C5 diagram

- page_id: `249575557`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575557/Creating+C5+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-04-29T10:22:26.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C5 Effects
- labels: ['create-c5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C5 diagram

1. Create a Logical or Physical Resource elements with Desired States.
2. Connect Capabilities and Desired States using Desired Effect relation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C5 diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Logical or Physical Resource elements with Desired States.</li><li>Connect Capabilities and Desired States using Desired Effect relation.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575562 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01338: Creating C7 Actual table

- page_id: `249575562`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575562/Creating+C7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T15:40:06.573+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C7 Performance Parameters' space='UPDM2P'] ), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the Add Existing button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the **Add Missing Measurements** button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C7 Actual table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new measurable Capability by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Capabilities that have at least one Measurement Set, defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="C7 Performance Parameters" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C7]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), and then specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells. </li><li>Add the existing Actual Property Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select the existing Actual Property Sets.</li><li><p>Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the <strong>Add Missing Measurements</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</p></li></ul><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575561 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01339: Creating C7 typical table

- page_id: `249575561`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575561/Creating+C7+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T14:02:40.041+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > C7 Performance Parameters
- labels: ['create-c7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C7 typical table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.
- Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the **Add Existing** button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C7 typical table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created. Type name, add Measurements, and edit Resources in the table cells.</li><li><p>Add an existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575565 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01340: Creating Cr diagram

- page_id: `249575565`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575565/Creating+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-03T12:23:35.280+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['create-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Cr diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** opens.

To create a Cr diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap** check box.

To create an Cr diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you choose to create Cr diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p>To create a Cr diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Cr diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575107 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01341: Creating CV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249575107`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575107/Creating+CV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:28:56.594+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-1 Vision
- labels: ['create-cv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355067341

1355067343

1355067342

The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.

**To create a CV-1 diagram**

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enterprise Phase' space='UPDM2P190'] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Vision' space='UPDM2P190'] and define a Vision Statement .
3. Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.
4. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P190'] .
5. Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.

An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.

In order to complete the CV-1, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75470d55-b66e-4a64-a044-071ad55ac81b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067341</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="336ed9a4-3300-4f1a-aefa-9f622956cfe2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067343</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ad3e002-77f7-4351-923f-d27aba4cfd99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355067342</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-1 should be the first product for DoDAF 2.0 architectures.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-1 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Enterprise Phase" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Vision" /></ac:link> and define a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Vision+Statement">Vision Statement</a>.</li><li>Associate the Vision with the Enterprise Phase.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P190" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capability and the Enterprise Phase using the Capability of Performer relationship.</li></ol><p>An additional Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Goals and the Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Enterprise Phase.</p><p>In order to complete the CV-1, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modelled to create the Capabilities that Enterprise Phase exhibits.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575110 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01342: Creating CV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249575110`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575110/Creating+CV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:39:27.301+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-cv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414337094

1414337097

1414337096

The CV-2 should be modeled just before the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P'] is completed.

**To create a CV-2 diagram**

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P'] .
2. Connect the Capabilities using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MDTWRT'] (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the *environmentConditions* property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f60e19b-dfe9-417d-8196-bee73d1c3818"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337094</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16baeadf-8566-46dd-b540-af4e734d28ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337097</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b759e35c-8b2a-4d3d-8610-3a60f9a0ffa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1414337096</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The CV-2 should be modeled just before the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-2 diagram</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Capabilities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Generalization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Performers and Resources using Capability of Performer relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the <em>environmentConditions</em> property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

When you choose to create CV-3 diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** opens.

To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create CV-3** check box.

To create an CV-3 diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** is disabled

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter.
4. The blank CV-3 chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575122 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01344: Creating CV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249575122`

1415279324

1415279323

The CV-4 is created just after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] is completed.

**To create a CV-4 diagram**

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities with Dependencies.

- page_id: `249575125`
