- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575125/Creating+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:22:55.718+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639449

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639451

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?

1415639450

The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.

A CV-5 table can be owned by a Capability Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Capability Phase element is related to the CV-1 view.

A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Capability of Performer.

**To create the CV-5 table**

1. Create an empty CV-5 table for the selected Enterprise Phase. How to create an empty CV-5 table?The owner of the CV-5 table can be only an Enterprise Phase.- Right-click a **CV-5** package as the diagram owner and select **Create Diagram** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the CV-5 table.- Right-click an Enterprise Phase and select **Create Diagram** > **UPDM CV Diagrams** > **CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping**. An empty table with the column number equal to associated Capabilities in the CV-1 view is created.
2. Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows. How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in CV-5 table?1. Click the**Add Rows**button.2. In the**Select Actual Organizational Resources** dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.3. Click **OK** when you are done.
3. Add Resources in table cells as table content. How to add a Resources in the CV-5 table?1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.2. Click the **+** button that appears at the right of the selected cell.[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment Milestones wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Deployment Milestones** wizard, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.4. Click **Finish** when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b656a146-6e29-4bfe-8629-c37f039821e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e7f9541-4099-4516-9fef-dfc12fa8ec5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639451</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26fddf02-da05-44a5-bb77-fa34b562fcde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415639450</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The content of the CV-5 table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575131 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01346: Creating CV-6 matrix

- page_id: `249575131`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575131/Creating+CV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:08.439+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-6-matrix', 'relate-operational-activity-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-6-matrix', 'build-cv-6-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416217377

1416217380

1416217379

A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.

The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.

**To create a CV-6 matrix**

1. Specify the Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.

**To map an Operational Activity to a Capability**

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b9a4e95-9372-4053-bfa2-e1046a32c83e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217377</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77bc8374-32db-4e62-b4dc-6ebaa9c8e309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217380</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6520e07-0424-4d41-81a6-10dc85407cab"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1416217379</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A CV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping matrix describes the mapping between Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that these Capabilities support.</p><p>The rows of the matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><strong>To create a CV-6 matrix</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Activity Part of Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p><strong>To map an Operational Activity to a Capability</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p><strong>To remove the relation</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416458510

1416458512

1416458511

The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Capabilities.

**To create a CV-7 matrix**

1. Specify Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Service Accesses expose Capabilities using the Capability of Performer relationship.

**To map a Service Access to a Capability**

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

**To remove the relation**

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575699 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01348: Creating D2 diagram

- page_id: `249575699`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575699/Creating+D2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-08T12:03:46.250+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint > D2 Deployed Resources
- labels: ['create-d2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P'] view.

To create an D2 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.
- the Persons and Actual Persons in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P'] diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an D2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Persons and Actual Persons in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576017 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01349: Creating Desired Effects

- page_id: `249576017`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576017/Creating+Desired+Effects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-08-24T07:36:57.867+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['select-states', 'create-desired-effect-relationship']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Desired Effect relationship connects the Desirer with the Operational / Resource state. If your Desirer has the state machine (Operational State Description or Resource State Machine), all the states are displayed in the Desirer compartments area.. This allows you to draw the Desired Effect relationship from the Desirer directly to the Operational / Resource state.

To view the states, click the Compartments smart manipulator and select States.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The states are not editable in the element compartment. When you want to edit states, you can do that in the model.
- Double-click the state to open the state diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose which states you want to see in the compartment area. 
 
 To select states

1. Right-click the element and select Edit Compartment.
2. Choose State tab in the **Compartment Edit** dialog.
3. Double-click or click the **>** button to select which states you want to see in the element compartment.
4. Click OK when you are done.

If one element has several state machine or region, you will see the names near the states in the element compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a desired effect relationship

1. Select a desirer and on smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='desired_effect_button.png'] .
2. Connect it to the desired state.

Additionally, you can create the desired effect relationship in the following ways:

- In the diagram pallet, click the Desired Effects button and connect the desirer to the Operational / Resource state.
- In the Model browser, select the desirer and from its shortcut menu, select New Relation > Outgoing > Desired Effect .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575193 space=UPDM2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 01350: Creating DIV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249575193`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575193/Creating+DIV-1+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:57:01.971+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint > DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-1-conceptual-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a DIV-1 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange Element' space=''] .
2. Connect Exchange Elements using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED'] .

You can also use a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model' space=''] to display Entities taxonomy. Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.

To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a DIV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="716e37c3-ad3c-46ae-8ea8-98198f927483">Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exchange Element" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exchange Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="ebbb1e66-7d1f-416d-8d5c-d93e329e50f1">Connect Exchange Elements using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Generalization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalizations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p>You can also use a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DIV-1 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to display Entities taxonomy. Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements.</p><p>To relate these UPDM Elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575196 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01351: Creating DIV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249575196`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575196/Creating+DIV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:42:47.906+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint > DIV-2 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-2-logical-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639577900

639577903

639577902

To create a DIV-2 diagram

1. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Item' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exchange Element' space='UPDM2P'] .
2. Add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Entity Attribute' space='UPDM2P'] for Entity Items.
3. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association of Information' space='UPDM2P'] relationships between Entity Items.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Details' space='UPDM2P'] relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45d1d971-c308-4b9c-906c-899c46c21395"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577900</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c29d24d-a915-441e-96ed-064d1f36bca3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577903</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c734e0d4-21bc-4554-9627-0929d8d33a5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639577902</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a DIV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Entity Item" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Exchange Element" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exchange Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Add <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Entity Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for Entity Items.</li><li>Create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Association of Information" /></ac:link> relationships between Entity Items.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Details" /></ac:link> relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575199 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01352: Creating DIV-3 diagram

- page_id: `249575199`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575199/Creating+DIV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:17:00.110+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Data and Information viewpoint > DIV-3 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-div-3-physical-data-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639773716

639773719

639773717

To create a DIV-3 diagram

1. Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.
2. Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dd57d01-c6ba-4bd9-a179-51df03ece8a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639773716</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0e5be14-638b-42b2-9254-1e378ae7e283"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639773719</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cdcc27f-4e80-4eca-9c60-2259a6fe5771"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639773717</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a DIV-3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li><li>Add Entity Attributes for Entity Items.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Entity Items or Exchange Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575702 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01353: Creating Dr table

- page_id: `249575702`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575702/Creating+Dr+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:08:59.852+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint > Dr Deployment Schedule
- labels: ['create-dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content of the Dr table are Resources used by Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period. Rows of the Dr table are Actual Organizational Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

A relationship between an Actual Organizational Resource and a Resource is expressed by the Deployed and No Longer Used Milestones. There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the resource is no longer used.

A Dr table can be owned by an Enterprise Phase that will be the context element for this table. The Enterprise Phases are modeled in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space='UPDM2P'] view.

A relationship between a Capability and a context element is represented by the Exhibits.

To create a Dr table

1. Create an empty Dr table for the selected Enterprise Phase. How to create an empty CV-5 table?The owner of the Dr table can be only an Enterprise Phase.1. Right-click a Dr package as the diagram owner and select **Create Diagram** > **Dr Deployment Schedule**. An Enterprise Phase is created automatically as the owner of the Dr table.
2. Add Actual Organizational Resources as table rows. How to add an Actual Organizational Resources in Dr table?1. Click the**Add Rows**button.2. In the**Select Actual Organizational Resources** dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.3. Click **OK** when you are done.
3. Add Resources in table cells as table content. How to add a Resources in a Dr table?1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.2. Click the **+** button that appears at the right of the selected cell.[IMAGE alt='' src='']3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment Milestones wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Deployment Milestones** wizard, select Resources and specify both deployment and no longer used dates for them.4. Click **Finish** when you are done.

The empty table with the column number equal to exhibited Capabilities in the selected time frame is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dr_table_add_resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating CV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p>3. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576104 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01354: Creating Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `249576104`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576104/Creating+Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T12:02:13.796+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Implementation Matrix
- labels: ['create-implementation-matrix', 'build-implementation-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Rows of the Implementation Matrix represent the implementation elements and the Columns represent the specification elements.

To create an Implementation Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (implementation elements).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (specification elements).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The implementation elements map to the specification elements by using the Implements relation.

To map the implementation elements to the specification elements

1. Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

The Implements relationship can be created according to the implementation rules:

- A System Resource can implement a Node/Performer.
- A Function can implement an Operational Activity.
- A Resource Interaction can implement an Operational Exchange.
- A Service Function can implement an Operational Activities
- A Service Function can implement a Function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Rows of the Implementation Matrix represent the implementation elements and the Columns represent the specification elements.</p><p>To create an Implementation Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (implementation elements).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (specification elements).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The implementation elements map to the specification elements by using the Implements relation.</p><p> </p><p>To map the implementation elements to the specification elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p>The Implements relationship can be created according to the implementation rules:</p><ul><li>A System Resource can implement a Node/Performer.</li><li>A Function can implement an Operational Activity.</li><li>A Resource Interaction can implement an Operational Exchange.</li><li>A Service Function can implement an Operational Activities</li><li>A Service Function can implement a Function.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575577 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01355: Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

- page_id: `249575577`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575577/Creating+L1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-04T11:25:07.166+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-high-level-operational-concept-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.

To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P'] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UPDM specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.<br /></span></p><p>To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

To create an L1 Node Types diagram

1. Create Logical Architectures or Nodes.
2. Connect Nodes using Node Associations.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575581 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01357: Creating L2 diagram

- page_id: `249575581`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575581/Creating+L2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-04-29T10:44:46.948+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['create-l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L2 diagram

1. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.
2. Associate Node Roles using Needlines.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Needlines.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575586 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01358: Creating L3 Node Interactions table

- page_id: `249575586`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575586/Creating+L3+Node+Interactions+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:09:18.316+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L3 Node Interactions
- labels: ['create-l3-node-interactions-table', 'add-operational-exchange-in-l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L3 Node Interactions table' space='UPDM2P'] Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an L1 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L3 Node Interactions table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L3 Node Interactions table" /></ac:link> Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">L1</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575590 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01359: Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `249575590`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575590/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-08T10:44:44.256+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P']Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram should be created first.

To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Logical Activities Taxonomy</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575591 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01360: Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249575591`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575591/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-08T10:48:42.870+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or reuse Nodes from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UPDM2P'] view (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or reuse Nodes from <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">view </span>(recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable of Performing relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575676 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01361: Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249575676`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575676/Creating+L4-P4+Systems+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T09:59:29.878+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'build-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</p></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575600 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01362: Creating L7 diagram

- page_id: `249575600`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575600/Creating+L7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T07:51:12.490+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L7 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L7 diagram

1. Create Exchange Elements or Entity Items.
2. Add Entity Attributes.
3. Create Entity Relationships.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Exchange Elements or Entity Items.</li><li>Add Entity Attributes.</li><li>Create Entity Relationships.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these <span>elements</span>, use Details relationship.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575603 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01363: Creating L8 table

- page_id: `249575603`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575603/Creating+L8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-08T11:17:40.609+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['create-l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L8 table, do either

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an L8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7">The new row in the table is created.</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Exchange Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission).</ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575609 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01364: Creating Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)

- page_id: `249575609`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575609/Creating+Lr+Lines+of+Development+Lr+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-13T14:52:51.269+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-lines-of-development', 'create-lr-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**opens.

To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Follow the steps of the `****` [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the******[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development** check box.

To create an Lr diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UPDM2P']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>opens.</p><p>To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Lr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><code><strong> </strong></code></span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eba5ac24-51bf-4a20-88c8-afd68a3afc79" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the<strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></strong></span> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create an Lr diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Lr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a5da39ff-0302-4822-b740-1df1ccaacb49" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575612 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01365: Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

- page_id: `249575612`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575612/Creating+Lr+Programme+Portfolio+Relationships+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T07:06:38.571+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-portfolio-relationships']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P']before creating the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.

To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole) . Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P']) or Actual Posts([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P']) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.

You can:

- Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.
- Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space='UPDM2P'] to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating <span>the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">(part)</span> to another<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (whole)</span>. Alternatively you can specify the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> whole and part </span>properties. <span> </span></li><li><p>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) o</span>r Actual Posts<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) r</span>esponsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b87a6e49-5998-4084-9df0-12dd640e4b24">Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Responsibility Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575610 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01366: Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249575610`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575610/Creating+Lr+Programme+to+Capability+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T08:29:41.009+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-to-capability-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lr Programme to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a Capability.

The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.

To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Column Scope (Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.

Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.

Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Lr Programme to Capability Mapping describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a Capability.</p><p>The rows of this matrix are Capabilities and the columns are Projects.</p><p>To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Activity is part of Project (“Activity Part of Project” relationship and a part of Capability (“Activity Part of Capability”) relationship). This transitive structure of relations maps Capabilities to Projects.</p><p>Project owns Increment Milestones that are related to Resources exhibiting capabilities.</p><p>Both transitive structures of relations map Capabilities to Projects. The mapping is displayed in the Matrix.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575611 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01367: Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix

- page_id: `249575611`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575611/Creating+Lr+Responsibility+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T08:33:11.113+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='UPDM2P']D2 diagrambefore creating the Lr Responsibility Matrix.

The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.

The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post).

To create the Lr Responsibilitymatrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b20a7230-c44c-4ae1-8fd6-2079374a1070" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">D2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility Matrix</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Projects and the columns represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post).</p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility </span>matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575578 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01368: Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `249575578`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575578/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L1+Node+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T13:54:39.577+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l1-node-types']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L1 Node Types diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Node Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Node Association on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P'] diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L1 Node Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> by doing one of the following:<span class="confluence-link"> </span><br /><ul><li>Select the Node Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange" ac:title="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Node Association on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.</p><p>The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="ee1ff623-8826-4c2a-9317-b7f80ae9f995" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575582 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01369: Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram

- page_id: `249575582`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575582/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L2+Logical+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T13:58:14.347+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l2-logical-scenario']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L2 Logical Scenario diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Needline and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Needline.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L2 Logical Scenario diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Needline and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange " ac:title="New Operational Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Needline.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7268b862-1271-4f2f-8573-5f9d91a9ef41" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575592 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01370: Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `249575592`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575592/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T14:06:11.105+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l4-logical-activities']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Operational Exchange**wizard . To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Operational Activity Edge.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange </strong>wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link"> </span>. To open the wizard, do either:<br /><ul><li>Select a Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange" ac:title="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the diagram pallet click <ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange" ac:title="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Operational Activity Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d55a547b-8d05-4c0e-97a0-bb3297855056" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575597 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01371: Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram

- page_id: `249575597`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575597/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L6+Logical+Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T14:08:47.109+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > L6 Logical Sequence
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l6-logical-sequence']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L6 Logical Sequence diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Operational Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - In the diagram pallet click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and select the Operational Message.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and connect the Lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575163 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01372: Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249575163`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575163/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:39:35.573+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Operational viewpoint > OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-ov-2', 'create-operational-exchange-in-operational-resource-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

898696775

898696777

898696776

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Associationbetween a pair of Nodes and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Nodes.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector between a pair of Node Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector .
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.
