<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575264 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01409: Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249575264`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575264/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T12:07:34.946+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Association</span> and on the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li>In the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a2aa563e-be67-452b-ae59-f97ff2723412" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575268 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01410: Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `249575268`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575268/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T12:03:57.012+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Resource Interface or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Resource Interface or Resource Connector.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eefa864d-0fcb-466e-ae3b-8d212a8147f6" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- page_id: `249575278`

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Function Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575231 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01415: Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description

- page_id: `249575231`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575231/Creating+Resource+Interaction+in+SvcV-4+Service+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-19T11:17:26.890+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-svcv-4-service-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Interaction in the SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_interaction.png'] .
  - On the diagram pallet, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction.png'] and then click the Function Edge.
2. Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an <span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span> in the SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram pallet, click <ac:image ac:alt="Resource Interaction" ac:title="Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Function Edge.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource Interaction, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="01e441ba-f595-4280-8b87-f4201a65392e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <strong><span Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span> Manager</strong></span></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog that enables to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource Interaction</span>s data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575626 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01416: Creating S1 diagram

- page_id: `249575626`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575626/Creating+S1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-15T07:23:31.367+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-s1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create S1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UPDM2P'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UPDM2P']. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create an S1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create S1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p>To create an S1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575633 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01417: Creating S4 Service Functions diagram

- page_id: `249575633`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575633/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-15T07:16:34.819+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an S4 Service Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575634 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01418: Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249575634`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575634/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T09:29:13.600+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575643 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01419: Creating S8 table

- page_id: `249575643`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575643/Creating+S8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T09:59:30.947+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['create-s8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create S8 table, do either

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).
- Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create S8 table<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either</span><span> </span></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).</li><li>Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576110 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01420: Creating Service Channel Summary Matrix

- page_id: `249576110`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576110/Creating+Service+Channel+Summary+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T12:04:35.285+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Service Channels Summary Matrix
- labels: ['create-service-channel-summary-matrix', 'build-service-channel-summary-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Service Channel Summary Matrix describes the relationships among services in a given Architectural Description.

The rows and the columns of this matrix are Service Interfaces.

To create a Service Channel Summary Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Services are connected with Resources using Service Channel connector. Both Service and Resource is type of Service Interfaces that are displayed as rows and columns of the Matrix. Rows are the types of Services and the columns are the types of Requests.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Service Channel Summary Matrix describes the relationships among services in a given Architectural Description.</p><p>The rows and the columns of this matrix are Service Interfaces.</p><p>To create a Service Channel Summary Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Services are connected with Resources using Service Channel connector. Both Service and Resource is type of Service Interfaces that are displayed as rows and columns of the Matrix. Rows are the types of Services and the columns are the types of Requests.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576107 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01421: Creating Service Channel Summary Table

- page_id: `249576107`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249576107/Creating+Service+Channel+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-28T13:44:47.817+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Service Channels Summary Table
- labels: ['create-service-channel-summary-table', 'build-service-channel-summary-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Service Channel Summary Table

1. Right-click the Package, select Create Diagram , point to UPDM Other Diagrams , and then click Service Channel Summary Table .
2. Fill in the table:

- Create a new Service Channel. Click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='New Service Channel wizard' space='UPDM2P']**New Service Channel** wizard opens. Follow the wizard steps to add a new Service Channel.
- Add an existing Service Channel. Clicking the Add Existing button. The Select Service Channel dialog opens. Only Service Channels are filtered.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Service Channel Summary Table</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Package, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, point to <strong>UPDM Other Diagrams</strong>, and then click <strong>Service Channel Summary Table</strong>.</li><li>Fill in the table:</li></ol><ul><li>Create a new Service Channel. Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="New Service Channel wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>New Service Channel</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Follow the wizard steps to add a new Service Channel.</li><li>Add an existing Service Channel. Clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The <strong>Select Service Channel</strong> dialog opens. Only Service Channels are filtered.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575321 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01422: Creating StdV-1 table

- page_id: `249575321`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575321/Creating+StdV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:42:34.161+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint > StdV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['create-stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-1 table, do either

- Add a new element by clicking the Add New button. In the Create element wizard select element and its owner.
- Add the existing elements by clicking the Add Existing button and in the Select UPDM element dialog select the elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the **…** button on any cell in the **Standard/Policy** column.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an StdV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new element by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. In the <strong>Create element</strong> wizard select element and its owner.</li><li>Add the existing elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select UPDM element</strong> dialog select the elements.</li><li><p>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575324 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01423: Creating StdV-2 table

- page_id: `249575324`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575324/Creating+StdV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T11:43:51.684+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint > StdV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['create-stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-2 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an StdV-2 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="782422b5-d615-450d-87e0-01fff970a0c1" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575256 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01424: Creating SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249575256`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575256/Creating+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T08:48:10.699+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

To create an SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Interaction Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575311 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01426: Creating SV-10a table

- page_id: `249575311`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575311/Creating+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T08:01:25.427+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-10a Systems Rules Model
- labels: ['add-resource-constraint-in-sv-10a', 'create-sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Systems Element (System, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization Type , Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).
- Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select constrained Systems Element (System,</span></span> Software,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span> Capability Configuration, Organization Type<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).</span></li><li>Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575263 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01427: Creating SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249575263`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575263/Creating+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-10T13:29:43.973+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] .
2. Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Resource Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>It is recommended to complete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating SV-2.</p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p>SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Resource Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575271 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01429: Creating SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `249575271`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575271/Creating+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:03:44.280+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-3-matrix', 'build-sv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p> </p><p>To create a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575276 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01430: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249575276`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575276/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:02:09.570+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'].
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575277 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01431: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `249575277`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575277/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:32:14.176+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.

diagram</span> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d062026e-79bd-468a-8246-2f709fcb55ba" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To display possible Resource Interactions, do either<br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select Function Edge and on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">smart manipulator</span> toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Interaction" ac:title="New Resource Interaction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_interaction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575282 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01432: Creating SV-5a matrix

- page_id: `249575282`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575282/Creating+SV-5a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:36:26.782+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5a-matrix', 'build-sv-5a-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5a-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575288 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01434: Creating SV-6 table

- page_id: `249575288`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575288/Creating+SV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-17T13:11:16.490+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-6', 'create-sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-6 table' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-6 table' space='UPDM2P'] Resource Interactions to fill in an SV-6 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-6 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-6 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in an SV-6 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="56cc6fe1-b993-4f00-b028-14d26f0a3763" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="76c11da5-a57b-4688-8734-3de2a02c662d" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575292 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01435: Creating SV-7 Actual table

- page_id: `249575292`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575292/Creating+SV-7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T12:29:30.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-measurable-resource-in-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-measures-in-sv-7-actual-table', 'add-missing-measure-types-in-sv-7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating SV-7 Actual table, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P'] must be created.

To create SV-7 Actual table, do either

- Add a new measurable Resource by clicking the Add New button and select one or more Resources that have at least one Measure Type, defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P'] ). Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measures or measurable Resources by clicking the Add Existing button and select the existing Measures or Resources.
- Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the Add Missing Measure Types button to update model changes to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="10b536d7-a3a8-48af-b2c4-26f6a0214908" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating SV-7 Actual table, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create SV-7 Actual table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new measurable Resource by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Resources that have at least one Measure Type, defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-7 Typical]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Specify the values of each Actual Measurement in the table cells.</li><li>Add the existing Measures or measurable Resources by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select the existing Measures or Resources.</li><li>Add the missing Actual Measurements by clicking the <strong>Add Missing Measure Types</strong> button to update model changes to the table.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575293 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01436: Creating SV-7 Typical table

- page_id: `249575293`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575293/Creating+SV-7+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T13:28:52.695+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-7 Typical table, do either

- Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. S elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.
- Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the **Add Existing**button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create SV-7 Typical table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Measurement Set by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">S</span>elect an owning element for the Measurement Set, and then specify the Measurements and Resources to be measured in the table cells.</li><li><p>Add the existing Measurement Sets by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select existing Measurement Sets from the model.</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575297 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01437: Creating SV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249575297`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575297/Creating+SV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-14T15:32:07.184+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-8 Systems Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-sv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button. New Version of Configuration is created.
2. Type its name.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**. 
 2. Select package where you want to create a resource. 
 3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category**Systems**,**Service**, or**Operational**, and select the desired resource.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone kinds defined:

- Increment Milestone (defines the start of a period) .
- Out Of Service Milestone (defines the end of a period).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. On the left of the resource Specification window, select the **Milestones**property group.
2. Click the **Add** button to define an Actual Project Milestone for the resource. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing Actual Project Milestone .
  - Create new Actual Project Milestone (described in the following procedure).
4. Click **Close** to close the Specification window of anActual ProjectMilestone.

To create new Actual Project Milestoneof the resource

1. In the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose an Actual Project Milestonecategory**Strategic**, or**Acquisition**, and select the desired milestone kind. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdmilestone specify two properties: [ATTACHMENT filename='milestone_specification.png']
  - Name. Type a name of the the milestone.
  - Date . Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575308 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01438: Creating SV-9 table

- page_id: `249575308`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575308/Creating+SV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T07:53:42.855+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint > SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-sv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-9 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Resource and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Resource as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Resource.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Resources, Standards, Protocols, or Competencies to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an SV-9 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Resource and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Resource as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Resource.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Resources, Standards, Protocols, or Competencies to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575212 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01439: Creating SvcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249575212`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575212/Creating+SvcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:42.052+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-1 Services Context Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984670277

984670279

984670278

You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'] views.

To create a SvcV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Accesses.
2. Associate Service Accesses using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Accesses mapping and Service Accesses interactions with the System Resources. You can model each Service Access internal structure using the SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P'] view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bde705a3-948f-455b-8612-7de7359d064c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670277</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fafcc91-61c6-41d4-aa18-2192f2b1d06d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670279</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e01dbbe-c69e-4742-8f31-f2d63925cdf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984670278</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ace814a8-de8a-4410-958f-537737ad7114"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SvcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Accesses.</li><li>Associate Service Accesses using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Interactions flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p>The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Accesses mapping and Service Accesses interactions with the System Resources. You can model each Service Access internal structure using the SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a097d0de-1300-4397-955b-7e384401ba57"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575203 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01440: Creating SvcV-10a table

- page_id: `249575203`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575203/Creating+SvcV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:49:29.143+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-10a Services Rules Model
- labels: ['create-svcv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SvcV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Systems Element (System, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization Type , Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).
- Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="wiki-content"><p>To create an SvcV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select constrained Systems Element (System,</span></span> Software,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span> Capability Configuration, Organization Type<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, Person Type, Function, Exchange Element, Entity Item, Resource Interaction).</span></li><li>Add existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button and select Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul><p> </p></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

3. Click the Rebuild button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575229 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01444: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249575229`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575229/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T08:32:23.033+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Service Accesses from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P'].
3. Use Is Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Service Accesses and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Service Accesses from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use<span style="color: rgb(0,51,0);"> Is </span>Capable of Performing relationship to connect the Service Accesses and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575230 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01445: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `249575230`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575230/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:07:43.460+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-flow-description', 'display-resource-interactions-in-svcv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='UPDM2P']SvcV-4 Service Functionality Descriptiondiagram should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Interactions on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Interactions, do either

- Select Function Edge and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].****
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Edge.The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
