<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575235 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01446: Creating SvcV-5 matrix

- page_id: `249575235`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575235/Creating+SvcV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:12:06.224+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-5-matrix', 'build-svcv-5-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix describes the mapping of services back to operational activities. The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Operational Activities.

To create a SvcV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Service Accesses).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Service Accesses maps to Operational Activities through transitive relationship. It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Service Accesses and Functions using Activity Performed by Performer relationship.

The SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix is non editable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix describes the mapping of services back to operational activities. The rows of this matrix are Service Accesses and the columns are Operational Activities.</p><p>To create a SvcV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Service Accesses).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Service Accesses maps to Operational Activities through transitive relationship. It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Service Accesses and Functions using Activity Performed by Performer relationship.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d9fc2455-1d5e-42cb-913c-154558a40fe3" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SvcV-5 Operational Activity to Services Traceability Matrix is non editable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575238 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01447: Creating SvcV-6 table

- page_id: `249575238`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575238/Creating+SvcV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:28:26.726+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-6', 'add-resource-interaction-in-svcv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-6 table' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-6 table' space='UPDM2P'] Resource Interactions to fill in an SvcV-6 table.

newTo add new Resource Interaction

1. In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Interaction

1. In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Interaction dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Interactions by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.

- Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] view to create or modify a Resource Interaction.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Resource Interaction Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-6 table" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-6 table" /></ac:link> Resource Interactions to fill in an SvcV-6 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="56cc6fe1-b993-4f00-b028-14d26f0a3763" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Interaction wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Interaction</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="76c11da5-a57b-4688-8734-3de2a02c662d" ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource Interaction</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SvcV-6 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Interaction</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource Interactions by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Interactions are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Identifier, Resource Interaction Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource Interaction.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Resource Interaction Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Interaction Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interaction data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575242 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01448: Creating SvcV-7 Actual table

- page_id: `249575242`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575242/Creating+SvcV-7+Actual+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:30:34.861+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-7-actual-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating a SvcV-7 Actual table, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P'] must be created first.

To create a SvcV-7 Actual table, do one of the following

- Add new measurable Service Access. Click the Add New button and select one or more Service Accesses that have at least one Measure Type defined (see SV-7 Typical). Specify values for each Actual Measurement - directly in the table cells.
- Add existing Actual Property Sets or measurable Service Accesses. Click the Add Existing button and select Existing Actual Property Sets or Service Accesses.
- Add missing Actual Measurements. Click the Add Missing Measure Types button to update table to model changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="575823a1-f081-4701-a563-18009d7ec956" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating a SvcV-7 Actual table, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-7 Typical table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-7 Typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SvcV-7 Actual table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add new measurable Service Access. Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select one or more Service Accesses that have at least one Measure Type defined (see SV-7 Typical). Specify values for each Actual Measurement - directly in the table cells.</li><li>Add existing Actual Property Sets or measurable Service Accesses. Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button and select Existing Actual Property Sets or Service Accesses.</li><li>Add missing Actual Measurements. Click the <strong>Add Missing Measure Types</strong> button to update table to model changes.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575243 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01449: Creating SvcV-7 Typical table

- page_id: `249575243`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575243/Creating+SvcV-7+Typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-18T10:30:54.627+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-7 Services Measures Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-7-typical-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-7 Typical table, do one of the following

- Add new Measure Type. Click the Add New button and select the owning element for Measure Type. Specify Measurements to the Measure Type and Service Accesses to be Measured - directly in the table cells.
- Add existing Measure Types. Click the Add Existing button and select Existing Measurement Types.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Existing Measurement Types.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 01450: Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249575247`

- labels: ['create-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram, do either

- Create or select existing Whole Life Configuration element and create its own SvcV-8 diagram.
- Create SvcV-8 diagram under the Package. The Whole Life Configuration as the owner is created automatically.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575252 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01451: Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `249575252`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575252/Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-10T12:40:09.588+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Services viewpoint > SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:
  - Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add New button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.
  - Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the Add Existing button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.
2. Add columns to the table by clicking the Time Period button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='UPDM2P']**Time Periods** dialog opens. Specify the forecast time periods:
  - Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.
  - Select the Forecast Kind.
  - Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.
3. Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the **…** button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast. By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a SvcV-9 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add Rows to the table by doing any of the following:<br /><ul><li>Adding a new Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add New</strong> button, select a Standard or Protocol and its owner.</li><li>Adding an existing Standard or Protocol as the row Header: click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select one or more existing Standards or/and Protocols.</li></ul></li><li>Add columns to the table by clicking the <strong>Time Period</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens. Specify the forecast time periods:<br /><ul><li>Select or create a Timeline package to store the Forecast dates.</li><li>Select the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Specify additional options needed according to the selected Forecast Kind.</li></ul></li><li><p>Enter the Subjects of Forecast by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on the cell you want to fill in and select the Standards and Protocols to forecast. Specify the date of the Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default the date is the starting date of the selected time period.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575105 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01452: CV-1 Vision

- page_id: `249575105`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575105/CV-1+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:40:04.589+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-1', 'cv-1-vision']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

845636083

845636085

845636084

**Description**

The CV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of capabilities. The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the

Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UPDM2P']. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.

**Implementation**

CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

CV-1 provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UPDM2P'].

845636082

**Related Pages:**

- Desired Effect
- Vision
- Vision Statement
- Capability
- Enterprise Phase

The purpose of a CV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architectural Description. It also provides a high-level scope for the</p><p>Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. CV-1 purpose is identical to MODAF StV-1 product usage. The difference between CV-1 model and StV-1 product lies in different concepts and relationships between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-1 can be represented using a CV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-1 Vision" ac:alt="CV-1 Vision"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>CV-1 provides a high-level scope for the Architectural Description which is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70ad0c4a-0d4d-48ba-b9e2-fea2e6d20c36"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">845636082</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f3c531f3-b22c-4b26-b549-0510bf602c45" /></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Desired+Effect">Desired Effect</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Vision">Vision</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Vision+Statement">Vision Statement</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Capability">Capability</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P/Enterprise+Phase">Enterprise Phase</a></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575108 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01453: CV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `249575108`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575108/CV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:07:48.506+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-2', 'cv-2-capability-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1354523297

1354523300

1354523299

**Description**

The CV-2 captures capability taxonomies. The model presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of a timeline – i.e., it can show the required capabilities for current and future capabilities.

The CV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

The intended usage of the CV-2 includes:

- Identification of capability requirements.
- Capability planning (capability taxonomy).
- Codifying required capability elements.
- Capability audit.
- Capability gap analysis.
- Source for the derivation of cohesive sets of user requirements.
- Providing reference capabilities for architectures.

**Implementation**

CV-2 can be represented using a CV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-2 is used to capture and organize the capability functions – required for the vision set out in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P'].

1354523296

Related Pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability of Performer' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Capability' space='UPDM2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575111 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01454: CV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `249575111`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575111/CV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:37:43.046+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-3', 'cv-3-capability-phasing']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1414409397

1414409399

1414409398

**Description**

The CV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e., capability phasing. The CV-3 supports the capability audit processes and similar processes used across the different COIs by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The CV-3 indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

The intended usage of the CV-3 includes:

- Capability planning (capability phasing).
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability gap analysis.

**Implementation**

CV-3 can be represented using a CV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The CV-3 can be presented as a table consisting of rows representing Capabilities (derived from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] model) and columns representing phases (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P'] model).

1414409396

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Increment Milestone' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Out of Service Milestone' space='UPDM2P190']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='UPDM2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575120 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01455: CV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `249575120`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575120/CV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:29:16.384+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-4', 'cv-4-capability-dependencies']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415019815

1415019818

1415019817

**Description**

The CV-4 describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities.

The CV-4 is intended to provide a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.

The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:

- Identification of capability dependencies.
- Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).

**Implementation**

CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This contrasts with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] model.

1415019814

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Property' space='UPDM2P']

The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management. In particular, the dependencies and groupings may suggest specific interactions between acquisition projects to achieve the overall capability.</p><p>The intended usage of the CV-4 includes:</p><ul><li>Identification of capability dependencies.</li><li>Capability management (impact analysis for options, disposal etc.).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>CV-4 can be represented using a CV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" ac:alt="CV-4 Capability Dependencies"><ri:attachment ri:filename="CV-4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This contrasts with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model which also deals with relationships between Capabilities; but <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> only addresses specialization-generalization relationship (i.e., capability taxonomy). However Capabilities used in CV-4 are usually created in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ac:link> model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07eaaf28-d857-4529-b88d-f0deb5988925"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1415019814</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="21d3b4f1-1d38-4fab-acf6-e2a01c6c1f0e" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="Capability Property" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249575123 space=UPDM2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 01456: CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping

- page_id: `249575123`
- space_key: `UPDM2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P/pages/249575123/CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-08-16T16:14:45.136+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint
- labels: ['cv-5', 'cv-5-capability-to-organizational-development-mapping']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415358073

1415358076

1415358075

**Description**

The CV-5 addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements.

This model shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular Capability Phase. The CV-5 can also be used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

The intended usage of the CV-5 includes:

- Fielding planning.
- Capability integration planning.
- Capability options analysis.
- Capability redundancy/overlap/gap analysis.
- Identification of deployment level shortfalls.

**Implementation**

CV-5 can be represented using:

- A CV-5 table.
- A CV-5 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To conduct a comprehensive analysis, several CV- 5 can be created to represent the different Enterprise Phases. Each CV-5 represents different Enterprise Phases defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space='UPDM2P'].

The CV-5 shows deployment of Capabilities defined in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space='UPDM2P'] to specific organizations.

Deployed or No Longer Used Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UPDM2P'].

Actual Organizational Resources are taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UPDM2P'].

The timing is based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UPDM2P'] indicating delivery of Capabilities to actual organizational resources, and also the point at which those organizational resources cease to use a particular Capability.

1415358072

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying CV-5 table' space='UPDM2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P']
